emails.subject: Re: Airbnb Reimbursement Request [CLSF-04213351] [HM2THC43B4]
emails.body:
Hi Miza,

Giulia sent me the following message when requesting a charge for damages - "Ho trovato la porta d'ingresso spalancata, per fortuna non manca niente nell'appartamento. Un po' più di attenzione la prossima volta", translated to stating that I had left the door open upon leaving the airbnb, but that nothing had been taken from the apartment. This is all the information I have regarding the "damage" she reported. I disputed the charge because (1) I do not think I left the door open, and (2) even if so, $1088 seemed like an exorbitant charge when the booking (after taxes) was approximately $60.

emails.subject: Re: [tickets.cs.columbia.edu #202359] Re: Fall 2025 New GRA Appointment
emails.body: Apologize for misspelling your name!
emails.snippet: Apologize for misspelling your name!
emails.originalBody:
Dear Students,

Welcome to Computer Science!

You will soon receive your appointment letter and hiring packet via
DocuSign.

While the packet contains detailed instructions, we want to highlight a few
important steps:

   1.

   Social Security Number (SSN)
   If you need to apply for an SSN, please see the attached instructions.
   Because it can take time to receive your Social Security receipt (required
   for processing your hiring paperwork), we have prepared the On-Campus
   Employer Letter for you in advance (Step 2). These letters are ready for
   pickup at the Computer Science Office (Computer Science Building, Suite
   450) Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM–5:00 PM.

   2.

   I-9 Verification
   You must complete both Part I (online) and Part II (in person), as this
   step authorizes you to work. Please refer to the attached instructions.

Please complete all parts of your hiring paperwork and submit it as soon as
possible, but no later than Friday, August 29, 2025.

If there are any changes or if you have been approved to receive a
fellowship recently, please let us know as soon as possible so we can
forward you relevant guidance and documentation.

Please don’t hesitate to let us know if you have any questions by emailing
student-payroll@cs.columbia.edu. We will also be available in person to
answer questions during the PhD Orientation on Monday, August 25, 2025.

Thank you,

Student Payroll

emails.snippet:
Hi Rob,

I hope you're doing well! I apologize for the delay in reaching out, but
I've been having t...
emails.originalBody:
Dear New PhD Students,

I am the Director of IT for the Department of Computer Science. The CS IT
team is Computing Research Facilities (CRF) and we will be working with you
during your time here.

We can be reached at crf@cs.columbia.edu.

Please read over the following items, especially the first one regarding CS
account creation.

1. Your Computer Science Account

We need to create your CS account.  Please note that if you were here
previously and already have an account with the same name as your UNI, you
should still apply for a new one and we will transfer your home directory
over.

Your account name can be whatever you like as long as it is available.

I’d like to encourage you to choose an account name that is both readable
and professional, keeping in mind that it will also be used for your CS
email (see item 2).  You can use your name (such as nancysmith or nsmith)
as long as it is not already in use.

Account request page: https://www.cs.columbia.edu/crf/account-form/ 

Notes on a few of the fields.

    Sponsor: Choose your advisor from the drop-down list.
    Account Type: Select "CS PhD Student."
    Username: Your choice.  We will contact you if it is unavailable.
    Password: Please choose a good one!

2. Your Computer Science Email

When your CS account has been set up I will submit a ticket to CUIT
(Columbia University IT) to have your CS email address created.  It will be
of the format username@cs.columbia.edu and will be an alias for your UNI
email address so you will be able to use the two of them interchangeably.

Columbia's mail service is called LionMail and runs on Google.  Information
about LionMail can be found here: https://cuit.columbia.edu/email/lionmail 

3. VPN account

Please follow the instructions on the CRF website if you need to use the
VPN (you must have a CS account first).

http://www.cs.columbia.edu  --> CRF (bottom of page) --> VPN

CUIT also provides a VPN service.

https://www.cuit.columbia.edu/remote-access-services 

4. Printing

Even if you already have installed a CS printer as an undergraduate or MS
student, please install it again using your CS PhD student account. There
is no printing limit for PhD student accounts. Please do not use the CS
printers for personal bulk print purposes such as printing flyers or books
that are not related to your research!

http://www.cs.columbia.edu  --> CRF -> Printers

5. Have a question or request?

Please send all your questions or requests via email to our ticketing
system:

    crf@cs.columbia.edu

If you require immediate assistance you can call the CRF hotline at
212-853-8426.

I think that's it. I will be stopping by the orientation meeting this
afternoon if you have any questions.

Welcome to the Computer Science department!  If you were previously a
student here, welcome back!

Regards,

Rob.

-- 
Rob Lane
Executive Director of Information Technology
Department of Computer Science, Columbia University
(212) 853-8402

emails.subject: Re: LLM-based social simulations
emails.body: That's great, does anytime Tuesday after 2:30PM work for everyone?
emails.snippet: That's great, does anytime Tuesday after 2:30PM work for everyone?
emails.originalBody:
Hi, Karthik, Lydia, Henry Lieberman, Research Scienist at MIT CSAIL, here. We had some conversations about your work in social simulations using LLMs, at IUI in Sardinia. You mentioned that you have a toolkit for doing such simulations and were
ZjQcmQRYFpfptBannerStart
This Message Is From an External Sender
This message came from outside your organization.
 
ZjQcmQRYFpfptBannerEnd
Hi, Karthik, Lydia,

Henry Lieberman, Research Scienist at MIT CSAIL, here. We had some 
conversations about your work in social simulations using LLMs, at IUI 
in Sardinia.

You mentioned that you have a toolkit for doing such simulations and 
were interested in collaborations.  I have some students and colleagues 
who are interested in working in this area, and we'd like to get a 
collaboration going. If you're willing to share your toolkit, it would 
be tremendously useful for us. We'd also be excited to learn of your 
future research directions and if there's any way we could help.

Some of the ideas we have in mind are:

* Variants on the Prisoner's Dilemma. In your IUI paper, you did a great 
simulation of the Prisoner's Dilemma along classic lines. We would like 
to experiment with variants of the rules, in order to arrive at 
scenarios that are more favorable to the evolution of cooperation. These 
might involve:
   - Relaxing the "no communication" rule between participants.
   - Modeling the long-term "wealth" of participants. We want to 
establish a relationship between scarcity/abundance and 
defection/cooperation. Wealthier participants can better afford to risk 
the chance of defection by partners, and so can reap a risk/reward 
benefit. So cooperating can actually be an "investment in the future", 
because it makes it more likely that your future partners will 
cooperate. Poorer participants would be unable to invest or take risk of 
loss.
   - Relaxing the "maximize score" goal.  Though technically the IPD is 
not a zero-sum game, any game with multiple players who are all trying 
to maximize their score makes it zero-sum in relation to the 
maximization goal (If I maximize, you can't). We would replace this with 
"satisficing" goals.
   - Being able to give individual participants different 
"personalities" or "strategies" that allow us to model heterogenous 
games, and condition results on the distribution of players with various 
strategies.

* Simulation of economic systems.  Our book, "Why Can't We All Just Get 
Along?" (https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.whycantwe.org&d=DwIDaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=434tGpy832-t3sYSYVcFqbdG-DHekr4UgHkBnu1IvxQY4TLH1P9OyeoNglzMCvM3&s=j4xJW6sQR43tUtIWVvbW4CNuHZB_P7qt8d7sfkDd5zE&e=) paints a vision of a post-scarcity 
future where universal abundance brought about by AI, personal 
manufacturing and biotech, promotes cooperation in social systems.  We 
propose a new economic principle called Makerism, based on more 
decentralized production made possible by technology. We would like to 
do simulations of Makerism vs. conventional Capitalism (or 
Communism/Socialism). We think we can debunk conventional arguments 
about "economies of scale" and "incentives", in the face of 
technological progress.  Please see our site, where you will find my 
12-minute TED talk as a quick introduction, and you may download the 
book free with coupon code: makerism. Please see the chapter on 
Makerism, Chapter 15.

* Education. We are working on a new interactive, "articulate book" 
edition of Why Can't We, and we'd like to link to online simulations 
that lets readers play interactively with the ideas. An LLM chatbot 
would be ideal as a simulation controller. We've also trained an LLM 
with the book content so that it can interactively answer questions and 
have conversations about the book ideas.

I'd like to introduce you to Aditya Mehta, cc'ed, who is an economist 
working with me.  He also has some economic ideas about strategies for 
small businesses who must compete with larger businesses that could also 
be tested with this methodology. If we could get Aditya started by 
direct contact with Karthik, he could see if we could get some of your 
tools going.  We also can set up a Zoom meeting to get acquainted.

We'd love to hear what you think about all this, and look forward to the 
possibility of working with you.

Best,

Henry Lieberman

Research Scientist
Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Lab (CSAIL)
Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
32 Vassar St. G 498
Cambridge, MA 02139 USA

Hello Karthik,

Thank you for reaching out and sharing your experience with the basement laundry room flooding. We understand how frustrating this must be, especially given your reliance on the in-building laundry facilities.

I've escalated your request to the property managers. We appreciate your patience as we work to resolve this issue.

Best regards,  
MD2 Support  
If your request is an emergency please call our emergency line 212-518-2690 ext 111.

On Thu, 4 Sep 2025 13:23:07 -0400 ks4190@columbia.edu wrote:

> Hi,
> 
> I’ve tried using the laundry room in the basement 4 times and it’s been completely flooded every time. I submitted a maintenance request before that was closed, but this is becoming unusable - I’ve been having to go to a laundromat despite choosing this apartment largely for the in-building washing. Please let me know if there is another place I can submit a request.
> 
> Thanks,
> Karthik

registration/PhD process!

Thanks,
Karthik

emails.originalBody:
Dear Tenants,

This is a general reminder to all tenants that rent is due on
the 1st of each month. We kindly ask you to ensure that your rent
payment is submitted on time. Timely payments are essential for maintaining
the smooth operation of the building and ensuring all services and
facilities continue to function effectively.

Please also remember to double-check that your Water/Sewer Fee is paid in
full alongside your rent.
If payment is not received by the 5th of the month, a late fee will be
applied to your account. To avoid this fee, we strongly encourage you to
submit your payment today or tomorrow if you have not already done so.

Your prompt attention to this matter is greatly appreciated.
Thank you for fulfilling this important responsibility.

Best Regards,

MD Squared Property Group

                        Office of Graduate Admissions                      

Dear Karthik,

                      You are receiving this email because it appears that we have not received all of your final official transcripts and/or proof of degree conferral.

                      For clarification, official and final transcript must have:                      	                           Confirmation that your degree was conferred or awarded, with the date                        
	                           Final grades for any class listed as in-progress during the admissions process                        

                      ​​
Graduating soon? You should not request your transcript until your degree is conferred.

                      The deadline to submit final official transcripts is Tuesday, October 15, 2025. You may review all outstanding items via your Enrollment Checklist <https://apply.engineering.columbia.edu/apply/status > and below.

If we do not receive your final official transcripts, a registration hold will be placed on your account for the Spring semester.

                      As a friendly reminder, we require certified and secure online official transcripts that are submitted directly to Columbia Engineering from your prior academic institution(s)' Registrar Office.

                      We are missing the following item(s) from you:

Official Transcript for University Of California, Berkeley

                      Please send your transcript electronically to finaldocs@columbia.edu | Columbia University, Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science. Transcripts sent to other offices at Columbia cannot be retrieved. Read more on Columbia Engineering's Official Transcript policy <https://www.gradengineering.columbia.edu/faq/admitted-students > on our website.

                      If you have questions about how to submit final official transcripts, please refer to our FAQ <https://www.gradengineering.columbia.edu/faq/admitted-students >. If you believe you have received this email in error, please email finaldocs@columbia.edu.

emails.originalBody:
Dear Karthik ,

Thank you for joining us today for the first day of *Engineering Your Ph.D.
(EYP) 2025*! We hope the sessions gave you a strong start to your doctoral
journey and introduced you to the people and resources that will support
you along the way.

As promised, attached are PDF versions of today’s presentations for your
reference.

*Tomorrow’s Schedule – Tuesday, August 26*

*Location: Carleton Commons (Mudd, 4th floor)*

   - 11:00 – 11:15 AM – Welcome and Brief Overview
   - 11:15 – 12:15 PM – CTL: Essentials of Teaching
   - 12:15 – 1:00 PM – Lunch
   - 1:00 – 2:00 PM – SEAS TA Panel
   - 2:00 – 3:00 PM – Inclusion and Belonging

*Looking Ahead to Thursday*

   -

   We will close EYP with a *Toast Reception* on *Thursday, August 28, from
   1:00 – 3:00 PM in Carleton Commons, *a chance to gather together and
   celebrate the start of your Ph.D. journey.
   -

   In addition, we are planning a *Thursday evening social event*. More
   details will follow soon. Stay tuned!

We look forward to seeing you back tomorrow as we continue with sessions on
teaching, the TA experience, and building a sense of belonging within the
Columbia Engineering community.

Best regards,
*Richard Hagen, Ph.D.* *(He/Him)*
Assistant Director, Graduate Student Affairs
Engineering Student Affairs
Columbia Engineering

--

emails.originalBody:
Hi Karthik!

My name is Julia and I'm the program coordinator at the Brown Institute.
Congrats on being selected as a winner for your project, "Making newsrooms’
institutional memory easily searchable by enabling conversational access!"
I'll be helping to facilitate your payment of $3,750 for your award. To
start, I'll send you an invitation to set up a Columbia vendor profile on a
platform called PaymentWorks. Could you please confirm:

1. Your full legal name (as written on tax documents)
2. A non-Columbia email I can use for the account

Thanks!
Julia

Hi,

Thanks for sharing, I got a little unexpectedly busy this week, but will
work on a script by th...
emails.originalBody:
The below is the latest prompt template

Generate a single HTML report of player charts from this JSON file.

General formatting

- Title = player name once (no initials).

- Everyone starts at 1000 and show the start time under the x-axis at the
first hand.

- Mark highest (black), lowest (red), and the final score (black if ≥1000,
red if <1000).

- At the highest point also add caption (small, left-aligned,
word-per-line):
The
Most
#
For
The
night

- At the lowest point add caption (small, left-aligned, word-per-line, in
red):

The
Lowest
#
For
The
night

- X-axis = EST with ticks every 20 minutes.

- Y-axis = ‘Points at end of every hand’ from 350 to 1450 in steps of 100.

- Remove any “Matplotlib Chart” text.

- Output a single self-contained HTML file (charts embedded as base64 PNG).

- One section per player, sorted alphabetically.

Event annotations (smaller fonts, stacked, left-aligned, no overlaps)

- Use small, non-bold font for all annotations (game names, Scoops, Quads,
Straight Flushes, deltas).

- Game names must be stacked one word per line and left-aligned.

- Placement rules:

   • Wins (Δ > 50) → place above the line.

   • Losses (Δ < -50) → place below the line.

- Always avoid overlapping labels: stagger left/right, jitter vertically,
and use angled arrows if crowded.

- Show delta under the game name, stacked: e.g.

Fifty
Three
2
Rows
+93
Scoops - Mark scoops as stacked label:
Scoop
+<points>
HH:M
- Prefer below the line if space, otherwise above.
- Include the exact EST time.

Quads

- For Quads, show the actual rank repeated four times, stacked, with points
and time, e.g.:

   KKKK

   +36

   23:28

- Parse from hand data, not just defaulting to AAAA.

Straight Flush
- Show as stacked, e.g.:
SF to Q
+72
22:14

- If top card < Q, just say “Straight Flush”.

- Always include points and time.

Implementation details

- Never overlap labels or arrows; if space is tight, angle arrows outward
and push labels left/right.

- All labels must remain readable and uncluttered.

waiting until the last minute. Most of our history of collaboration is in
my inbox.

I'd love to be able to 1) find all the emails related to the grant and 2)
analyze them to see how and went we actually did the work... so for next
time I could have a better estimate of the time it takes and our cadence
for working.

Not necessarily an immediate thing, just a goal to keep on the list :)

emails.subject: Re: Fw: Resident Memo – Community Guidelines & Building Security
emails.body: yea i get all these thanks though!
emails.snippet: yea i get all these thanks though!
emails.originalBody:
Dear Residents,

Management's  committed  to maintaining a safe and comfortable living
environment. Residents are urged to follow these important guidelines:

   - *Be mindful of noise levels*—please refrain from playing loud music or
   TV between 9:30 PM and 9:00 AM
   - *Clean up after yourself *in all shared spaces, including hallways,
   staircases, and laundry rooms.
   - *Dispose of garbage and recycling properly*—please use designated bins
   and avoid leaving trash in hallways or common areas.
   - *Smoking is strictly prohibited anywhere on the premises.* Violations
   will result in a $250 fine.
   - *Do not buzz in anyone you do not personally know.* Unauthorized entry
   compromises the safety of the entire building.
   - *Ensure the front door fully closes and locks behind you* when
   entering or exiting the building.
   - *If you notice someone inside the building who appears unauthorized*,
   report it to management immediately.
   - *Package safety:* If you are expecting a delivery, retrieve it as soon
   as possible to help prevent theft. If your building has a designated
   package room, please ensure the door is securely closed after use.

We appreciate your cooperation in keeping our community safe and secure.
If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to reach out.

Best Regards,
MD Squared Property Group

emails.originalBody:
Folks:

This morning I'll be referring to the syllabus from my own section of CC.
Please see attached.

Also, we will look at some examples of CC midterm questions. Here's one
below.

Best

Matt

Identify the following text -- where it comes from and the significance of
the passage within its argumentative context. Your answer should consist of
one shortish paragraph (equivalent to no more than one side of one page in
the blue book). Identify the passage by number. (13 pts.; 3 for
identification, 10 for significance.)

“Every art and every inquiry, and similarly every action and pursuit, is
thought to aim at some good; and for this reason the good has rightly been
declared to be that at which all things aim. But a certain difference is
found among ends; some are activities, others are products apart from the
activities that produce them. Where there are ends apart from the actions,
it is the nature of the products to be better than the activities.”

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Matthew Connelly

Professor of History

Vice Dean for AI Initiatives

403 Fayerweather Hall

Department of History

Mail Code 2508

1180 Amsterdam Avenue

Columbia University

New York, NY 10027

(212) 854-4563

emails.subject: Compensation and Stipend Increases
emails.body:
Dear Student,

Please see the below October 6, 2025, communication from the University
regarding increases to student support.  Depending upon whether you receive
wages or stipend, your increase will be in your paycheck or your student
account.

Should you have any questions regarding your payment, please follow you
with your academic department.

Best regards,
SEAS Grad Funding

*From: *Office of Public Affairs <columbianews@m.columbia.edu>
*Reply-To: *Office of Public Affairs <
reply-fec715767d61077c-1318_HTML-57385648-546002910-3003@m.columbia.edu>
*Date: *Monday, October 6, 2025 at 9:46 PM
*Subject: *Compensation and Stipend Increases

Columbia will implement the following increases to student support, applied
pro rata effective October 16, 2025.

[image: Columbia University logo]

[image: A view looking over Alma Mater’s shoulders across campus to Butler
Library.]

*October 6, 2025*

Dear Students and Faculty,

As you are aware, the University and the Student Workers of Columbia union
(SWC) have made little progress in negotiating a successor contract since
our first and only bargaining session in March. Information on our most
recent bargaining attempt
<https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=341c47e64254658159304d035a980065daca9c4bba43f394e39c4e741337bf5855b8ff4fb976d0f34fb6b8ae5b7f485fdce22f04b1ed8eb3c51574ae4cac4532 >
is available. Still, the University remains fully committed to supporting
our graduate student community and student employees.

Columbia has some of the finest young scholars in the world, who are
focused on their coursework, teaching, and research, and who contribute
much to making Columbia the great institution that it is. Accordingly,
although the University continues to navigate difficult financial headwinds
that will persist for the foreseeable future, Columbia *will implement the
following increases to student support, applied pro rata effective October
16, 2025*:

   - *Compensation will increase by 3%* for PhD student employees who hold
   a teaching or research appointment in a guaranteed year of support in GSAS,
   SEAS, the VP&S Coordinated Doctoral Programs in Biomedical Sciences, the
   School of Nursing, the Columbia Business School, the Mailman School of
   Public Health, GSAPP, Journalism, SIPA, and the School of Social Work. This
   increase will apply to the pay period beginning October 16, which is paid
   on November 7. PhD students receiving stipends at an on-appointment rate,
   including those beyond their guaranteed years of funding who have been
   released from teaching duties, will also receive 3% increases. Stipends
   payments will be issued the week of October 27, reflecting the increase for
   fall, prorated from the October 16 effective date.

   - PhD students not on appointment in guaranteed years of funding *will
   also receive 3% increases* to their minimum support stipends. Stipends
   payments will be issued the week of October 27, reflecting the increase for
   fall, prorated from the October 16 effective date.

   - In each academic unit, compensation * will increase by 3%* for
   undergraduate students, Master’s students, non-PhD doctoral students, and
   PhD students beyond their guaranteed years of support who hold appointments
   as Teaching Assistants, Teaching Fellows, Preceptors, Readers, Teaching
   Assistants III, Graduate Research Assistants and Department Research
   Assistants. This increase will apply to the pay period beginning October
   16, which is paid on November 7.

   - The University will also *increase the minimum hourly rate* for
   student employees who perform instructional or research work from
   $22.50/hour to $23.00/hour, starting October 20, 2025.

With the academic year now well underway, the University is disappointed
that the union has not returned to the bargaining table or responded to the
University’s proposals. Most recently, after canceling an agreed-upon
bargaining session on September 11, without explanation, SWC refused to
bargain at a scheduled meeting today, October 6. The union has posted that
the University “canceled” today’s session. The University’s bargaining team
was present at the neutral Interchurch location from the agreed-upon start
time and remained ready to bargain. SWC’s delegation arrived an hour late
to present demands but would not stay to negotiate or discuss scheduling
future sessions, asking instead for email replies. The full update on the
scheduled October 6 bargaining session
<https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=341c47e6425465814316b759a22f869e97d1b139590debad1e8ebc5f35410a188bb4da170daae4caa9f162fb6214bce74f3b305ba8c5daf70a35bd3a8b123f33 >
is available.

Additional information regarding negotiations is available under the Current
Negotiations
<https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=341c47e6425465819237c7b8c46a9e99e2d0d51f24b4c38df11640c6adce8c9c2cdec81af891f1970c10e3f9309fc9b9525c7c4616b44f4e3735eba1596596a5 >
tab on the Student Benefits website.

Thank you for everything you do to support our community and advance
Columbia’s teaching and research mission.

Sincerely,

Amy Hungerford
Dean and Executive Vice President, Faculty of Arts and Sciences

Cas Holloway
Special Advisor to Acting President Claire Shipman

*Copyright © 2025 Columbia University. All rights reserved.*

You are receiving this email because you are part of the Columbia
University community.

*Columbia University - Office of Public Affairs*
402 Low Library, MC 4321, 535 W. 116th St
New York, NY 10027

*Confidentiality Notice:*

*The information contained in the email message is intended only for the
personal and confidential use of the recipient(s) named above.  If the
reader of this message is not the intended recipient or an agent
responsible for delivering it to the intended recipient, you are hereby
notified that you have received this document in error and that any review,
dissemination, or copying of this message is strictly prohibited.  If you
have received this communication in error, please notify me immediately by
email, and delete the original message.*

is available. Still, the University remains fully committed to supporting
our graduate student community and student employees.

Columbia has some of the finest young scholars in the world, who are
focused on their coursework, teaching, and research, and who contribute
much to making Columbia the great institution that it is. Accordingly,
although the University continues to navigate difficult financial headwinds
that will persist for the foreseeable future, Columbia *will implement the
following increases to student support, applied pro rata effective October
16, 2025*:

   - *Compensation will increase by 3%* for PhD student employees who hold
   a teaching or research appointment in a guaranteed year of support in GSAS,
   SEAS, the VP&S Coordinated Doctoral Programs in Biomedical Sciences, the
   School of Nursing, the Columbia Business School, the Mailman School of
   Public Health, GSAPP, Journalism, SIPA, and the School of Social Work. This
   increase will apply to the pay period beginning October 16, which is paid
   on November 7. PhD students receiving stipends at an on-appointment rate,
   including those beyond their guaranteed years of funding who have been
   released from teaching duties, will also receive 3% increases. Stipends
   payments will be issued the week of October 27, reflecting the increase for
   fall, prorated from the October 16 effective date.

   - PhD students not on appointment in guaranteed years of funding *will
   also receive 3% increases* to their minimum support stipends. Stipends
   payments will be issued the week of October 27, reflecting the increase for
   fall, prorated from the October 16 effective date.

   - In each academic unit, compensation * will increase by 3%* for
   undergraduate students, Master’s students, non-PhD doctoral students, and
   PhD students beyond their guaranteed years of support who hold appointments
   as Teaching Assistants, Teaching Fellows, Preceptors, Readers, Teaching
   Assistants III, Graduate Research Assistants and Department Research
   Assistants. This increase will apply to the pay period beginning October
   16, which is paid on November 7.

   - The University will also *increase the minimum hourly rate* for
   student employees who perform instructional or research work from
   $22.50/hour to $23.00/hour, starting October 20, 2025.

With the academic year now well underway, the University is disappointed
that the union has not returned to the bargaining table or responded to the
University’s proposals. Most recently, after canceling an agreed-upon
bargaining session on September 11, without explanation, SWC refused to
bargain at a scheduled meeting today, October 6. The union has posted that
the University “canceled” today’s session. The University’s bargaining team
was present at the neutral Interchurch location from the agreed-upon start
time and remained ready to bargain. SWC’s delegation arrived an hour late
to present demands but would not stay to negotiate or discuss scheduling
future sessions, asking instead for email replies. The full update on the
scheduled October 6 bargaining session
<https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=341c47e6425465814316b759a22f869e97d1b139590debad1e8ebc5f35410a188bb4da170daae4caa9f162fb6214bce74f3b305ba8c5daf70a35bd3a8b123f33 >
is available.

Additional information regarding negotiations is available under the Current
Negotiations
<https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=341c47e6425465819237c7b8c46a9e99e2d0d51f24b4c38df11640c6adce8c9c2cdec81af891f1970c10e3f9309fc9b9525c7c4616b44f4e3735eba1596596a5 >
tab on the Student Benefits website.

Thank you for everything you do to support our community and advance
Columbia’s teaching and research mission.

Sincerely,

Amy Hungerford
Dean and Executive Vice President, Faculty of Arts and Sciences

Cas Holloway
Special Advisor to Acting President Claire Shipman

*Copyright © 2025 Columbia University. All rights reserved.*

You are receiving this email because you are part of the Columbia
University community.

*Columbia University - Office of Public Affairs*
402 Low Library, MC 4321, 535 W. 116th St
New York, NY 10027

*Confidentiality Notice:*

*The information contained in the email message is intended only for the
personal and confidential use of the recipient(s) named above.  If the
reader of this message is not the intended recipient or an agent
responsible for delivering it to the intended recipient, you are hereby
notified that you have received this document in error and that any review,
dissemination, or copying of this message is strictly prohibited.  If you
have received this communication in error, please notify me immediately by
email, and delete the original message.*

Dear residents, 

NYC is expecting inclement weather tomorrow, October 30, with rainfall anticipated to reach up to one inch and wind gusts up to 20-30mph. 

In preparation for the weather, and to ensure everyone’s safety, please remember to keep all windows shut, and move any outdoor furniture indoors and off of terraces, patios, and roof areas.  

If you experience any issues, please don't hesitate to reach out to your management team. 

Please also note the emergency line that can be reached anytime at (212) 518- 2690 ext 111. Our support center is also available at all times at support@md2pg.com. 

Thank you for your attention, enjoy your weekend, and please let us know if you have any questions or need anything.

Best regards,
MD2 Property Group

emails.subject: Thank you for your Application!
emails.body:
Hi, Karthik, 

Thank you for applying to the Research Intern - AI Agents (Job number:1903410) position at Microsoft!  We're glad you're interested in a career at Microsoft and we're here to help you find a perfect fit.
 
You may not receive feedback from us on your application directly, but please know that it’s being evaluated, and you’ll hear from us as soon as the review process is complete. If you’re selected for an interview, you’ll be notified by someone on the recruiting team.
 
You can view your application status updates through your Action Center. If you see the job moved to an Archived state, that means the position is either no longer open, you withdrew from consideration, or you were not selected for the role.
 
You may notice that we move your application from one role to another. This may happen a few times and is a normal part of our recruiting process. So, if you see the job you applied to in an Archived state, and a new job listed as Active, please know that this is normal and does not negatively impact your candidacy in any way.
 
How’s your profile?
A key part of the review process is evaluating your profile in relation to the job requirements, so please make sure your profile is accurate and extensive – it’s our first step in getting to know you! You can build your profile anyway you’d like – you can import it from LinkedIn, manually update it, or import/attach a resume. The most important thing is that your profile tells your story!
 
We encourage you to check back frequently and continue to look for opportunities that match your interests, as new jobs are being posted regularly.
 
Thank you,
Microsoft Recruiting

This mail is sent from an unmonitored mailbox. Please do not reply. 
Microsoft respects your privacy. To learn more, please read our Microsoft Data Privacy Notice.

emails.subject: Happy Halloweek 🎃🕸️👻 | EGSC Weekly Newsletter
emails.body:
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/  >
*Hey Grad SEAS!*
Spooky season is here, and we’re celebrating this *Friday *at our* EGSC
Halloween Social*! Join us for festive *treats*, spooky *activities*, and
hauntingly *good vibes* with your fellow grad students 🎃👻

Don't forget to *follow us on Instagram @columbiaegsc
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.instagram.com/columbiaegsc/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg9getv3k$ > *to stay updated on all our
events!

*👻 Halloween Social 🎃*
 Join us for a spook-tacular afternoon of fall festivities!
📆 *Date: *October 31
⏰ *Time: *12:00 PM – 3:00 PM
📍 *Location:* Back of Carleton Commons
Come build a haunted Lego masterpiece, decorate your own pumpkin, and enjoy
cozy treats like hot cider, hot chocolate, and pie crust cookies.
Take a break from debugging, and start *de-BOO-gging* instead!! 🕸️🧡
Join us at our *weekly GBMs*, whether you are a Department Representative
or not! We meet on Fridays from 4-5 pm in Mudd 524.

This week, we will be joined by *CPS* and *Stan Sakry, Senior Director of
Facilities and Space Planning*!

*Lunch & Learn: LGBTQ+ History Month Edition*
Come grab lunch and celebrate LGBTQ+ History Month with us! While you eat,
enjoy a fun slideshow of surprising facts, trailblazing figures, and
milestones from LGBTQ+ history!

*Join the ELC Executive Board 2026!*
The Entrepreneurship Leadership & Consulting (ELC) Club is one of Columbia
Engineering’s oldest and most dynamic student organizations—connecting
aspiring entrepreneurs and future consultants through hands-on events,
speaker series, and startup collaborations.

They're looking for passionate students to join our 2026 Executive Board!
Open positions include VP Entrepreneurship, VP Management Consulting, VP
Tech Consulting, VP Operations, and VP External Affairs.

📅 Deadline: *October 31st, 11:59 PM*
🔗 Apply Here
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://forms.gle/xrtSTVBt23YSbwby7__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!XlQ6LxSGX7Asj52kkRQ_41EAnyPCGA9MeJiJeWIarXme4l0cxxsMQUAXB5EFTeuSzUEw6cb9ZQ7ZOp9jz4CTqfVTAkh6Tx2LSAKquFQ$>
Come join the *Columbia Entrepreneurship & Venture Society* for their *General
Body Meeting* this *Wednesday* at *11:00 a.m.* in *Uris 326*! They'll be
discussing upcoming events and ways to get involved in the club. Whether
you’re a returning member or new to CEVS, they'd love to see you there.
Meetings are held *every other Wednesday*, so don’t miss out on the chance
to connect, share ideas, and grow your network!

*The First-Ever Columbia Product Hackathon 2025 is here!* 🚀

Hosted by the *Columbia Product Managers Club (PMC)* in collaboration with
the *NYU Product Management Club (NYU PMC)* — and joined by *Wharton,
Cornell, Duke, Northeastern University, Boston University, Carnegie Mellon
University (CMU), University of Southern California (USC), and UC Berkeley* —
this event brings together the brightest product minds from top schools for
an epic, cross-campus innovation challenge.

Over *48 hours*, teams will *think, build, and pitch* product solutions to
real-world problems.

📅 *Event Timeline*
💻 *Nov 3:* Online Kickoff & Prompt Release
🏫 *Nov 4:* In-Classroom Round @ Columbia (Mudd 1127) at *6 PM*
🏁 *Nov 6:* National Semi-Finals @ NYU Campus
🏆 *Nov 9:* National Finals @ NYU Campus

🕓 *Application Deadline: Oct 29, 2025 (EOD)* — don’t miss your shot to
represent Columbia!

💰
*$3,000+ Prize Pool🎯 Mentorship *from top PM leaders in tech & consulting
*📜 Certificates, swag, and national recognition at NYU*

📝 *Sign up now — we’ll share more details after you register!*
Google Form Link:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdOB91NpIxAMv0nuJbi6CjF-mvPQ1GPKI8rcYBtFbJ6MvDzYA/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgAShtI5Q$ 
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdOB91NpIxAMv0nuJbi6CjF-mvPQ1GPKI8rcYBtFbJ6MvDzYA/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!VQBWTHLuV9S-vvKJg04JMYFEtMFDjEwDLWHH5dnXQpYDFZ1Y3qJ7GAUsU1KhqNBknk2U4G5JzcXoEZmfKokT1B4nfEfsXCfxorkgZ2Y$>
or scan the *QR code* on the poster.

📩 More details and team confirmations will be shared via email after
registration.
⚠️ *Registrations close October 29, EOD*.

💡 Let’s make history, Lions — *build, innovate, and lead the future of
product!* 🦁🔥

*Upcoming Columbia Business School Events!*

   - Weds, Nov 5, 6-7pm. Lang Talk with Cyrus Massoumi, founder of
Zocdoc. *RSVP
   here. <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/ia6dvjbz__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgv2bokeg$ >*
   - Weds, Nov 12, 6-8:30pm. CBS Big Idea Pitch Competition and Networking
   event. *RSVP link pending.*
   - Thurs, Nov 13, 12:30-2:30pm. CBS Tabling: Taste & Shop at CBS. *RSVP
   here. <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/nvrzzvpv__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgN9vDTCc$ >*
   - Fri, Nov 14, 10-1:30pm*. *The AI Advantage: Building and Funding the
   Next Wave of Startups. *RSVP here. <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/7nwiifge__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgCzFTlUM$ >*

*The  <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >**Spring
2026 CBL Engineering application
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >** is now open!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >
*Columbia Build Lab (CBL)
<https://business.columbia.edu/lang/student-and-alumni-programming/launch/columbia-build-lab  >*
matches
Columbia students pursuing Computer Science (or related degrees) with CBS
student-founders of an early-stage startup. Engineers in the Build Lab
program gain experience working to solve real-world challenges with teams
of peers and CBS founders. This is an excellent opportunity for any
engineering or computer science student looking to gain valuable experience
over the summer!
Submit your application to the Spring 2026 cohort by December 7!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >
Interested in joining *Columbia’s only student club dedicated to
Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI)*? At *Neurotech X Columbia*, you’ll find
opportunities to engage in cutting-edge research projects  and connect with
peers passionate about neurotechnology. We welcome all members of the
Columbia community to join us — please register through this Google Form:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/1r6jLNyCo6pbxXR3jEqPT0a_FzcpqlB4d4iCH3_75ZbQ__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgSbHABoA$ 
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/1r6jLNyCo6pbxXR3jEqPT0a_FzcpqlB4d4iCH3_75ZbQ__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!U66AXcO3CK09cjy5CEinktcs0lyktt1ow3JxvVucDXBZcW_LeYTqdaFHhq0nJeCzhWoF8A1KI_e8L7oaewiVfhUko8lKWg$>
[image: Instagram] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://instagram.com/columbiaegsc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgIWl_f0k$ >

📍 *Location:* Back of Carleton Commons
Come build a haunted Lego masterpiece, decorate your own pumpkin, and enjoy
cozy treats like hot cider, hot chocolate, and pie crust cookies.
Take a break from debugging, and start *de-BOO-gging* instead!! 🕸️🧡
Join us at our *weekly GBMs*, whether you are a Department Representative
or not! We meet on Fridays from 4-5 pm in Mudd 524.

This week, we will be joined by *CPS* and *Stan Sakry, Senior Director of
Facilities and Space Planning*!

*Lunch & Learn: LGBTQ+ History Month Edition*
Come grab lunch and celebrate LGBTQ+ History Month with us! While you eat,
enjoy a fun slideshow of surprising facts, trailblazing figures, and
milestones from LGBTQ+ history!

*Join the ELC Executive Board 2026!*
The Entrepreneurship Leadership & Consulting (ELC) Club is one of Columbia
Engineering’s oldest and most dynamic student organizations—connecting
aspiring entrepreneurs and future consultants through hands-on events,
speaker series, and startup collaborations.

They're looking for passionate students to join our 2026 Executive Board!
Open positions include VP Entrepreneurship, VP Management Consulting, VP
Tech Consulting, VP Operations, and VP External Affairs.

📅 Deadline: *October 31st, 11:59 PM*
🔗 Apply Here
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://forms.gle/xrtSTVBt23YSbwby7__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!XlQ6LxSGX7Asj52kkRQ_41EAnyPCGA9MeJiJeWIarXme4l0cxxsMQUAXB5EFTeuSzUEw6cb9ZQ7ZOp9jz4CTqfVTAkh6Tx2LSAKquFQ$>
Come join the *Columbia Entrepreneurship & Venture Society* for their *General
Body Meeting* this *Wednesday* at *11:00 a.m.* in *Uris 326*! They'll be
discussing upcoming events and ways to get involved in the club. Whether
you’re a returning member or new to CEVS, they'd love to see you there.
Meetings are held *every other Wednesday*, so don’t miss out on the chance
to connect, share ideas, and grow your network!

*The First-Ever Columbia Product Hackathon 2025 is here!* 🚀

Hosted by the *Columbia Product Managers Club (PMC)* in collaboration with
the *NYU Product Management Club (NYU PMC)* — and joined by *Wharton,
Cornell, Duke, Northeastern University, Boston University, Carnegie Mellon
University (CMU), University of Southern California (USC), and UC Berkeley* —
this event brings together the brightest product minds from top schools for
an epic, cross-campus innovation challenge.

Over *48 hours*, teams will *think, build, and pitch* product solutions to
real-world problems.

📅 *Event Timeline*
💻 *Nov 3:* Online Kickoff & Prompt Release
🏫 *Nov 4:* In-Classroom Round @ Columbia (Mudd 1127) at *6 PM*
🏁 *Nov 6:* National Semi-Finals @ NYU Campus
🏆 *Nov 9:* National Finals @ NYU Campus

🕓 *Application Deadline: Oct 29, 2025 (EOD)* — don’t miss your shot to
represent Columbia!

💰
*$3,000+ Prize Pool🎯 Mentorship *from top PM leaders in tech & consulting
*📜 Certificates, swag, and national recognition at NYU*

📝 *Sign up now — we’ll share more details after you register!*
Google Form Link:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdOB91NpIxAMv0nuJbi6CjF-mvPQ1GPKI8rcYBtFbJ6MvDzYA/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgAShtI5Q$ 
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdOB91NpIxAMv0nuJbi6CjF-mvPQ1GPKI8rcYBtFbJ6MvDzYA/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!VQBWTHLuV9S-vvKJg04JMYFEtMFDjEwDLWHH5dnXQpYDFZ1Y3qJ7GAUsU1KhqNBknk2U4G5JzcXoEZmfKokT1B4nfEfsXCfxorkgZ2Y$>
or scan the *QR code* on the poster.

📩 More details and team confirmations will be shared via email after
registration.
⚠️ *Registrations close October 29, EOD*.

💡 Let’s make history, Lions — *build, innovate, and lead the future of
product!* 🦁🔥

*Upcoming Columbia Business School Events!*

   - Weds, Nov 5, 6-7pm. Lang Talk with Cyrus Massoumi, founder of
Zocdoc. *RSVP
   here. <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/ia6dvjbz__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgv2bokeg$ >*
   - Weds, Nov 12, 6-8:30pm. CBS Big Idea Pitch Competition and Networking
   event. *RSVP link pending.*
   - Thurs, Nov 13, 12:30-2:30pm. CBS Tabling: Taste & Shop at CBS. *RSVP
   here. <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/nvrzzvpv__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgN9vDTCc$ >*
   - Fri, Nov 14, 10-1:30pm*. *The AI Advantage: Building and Funding the
   Next Wave of Startups. *RSVP here. <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/7nwiifge__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgCzFTlUM$ >*

*The  <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >**Spring
2026 CBL Engineering application
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >** is now open!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >*

*Columbia Build Lab (CBL)
<https://business.columbia.edu/lang/student-and-alumni-programming/launch/columbia-build-lab  >*
matches
Columbia students pursuing Computer Science (or related degrees) with CBS
student-founders of an early-stage startup. Engineers in the Build Lab
program gain experience working to solve real-world challenges with teams
of peers and CBS founders. This is an excellent opportunity for any
engineering or computer science student looking to gain valuable experience
over the summer!
Submit your application to the Spring 2026 cohort by December 7!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >
Interested in joining *Columbia’s only student club dedicated to
Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI)*? At *Neurotech X Columbia*, you’ll find
opportunities to engage in cutting-edge research projects  and connect with
peers passionate about neurotechnology. We welcome all members of the
Columbia community to join us — please register through this Google Form:

emails.subject: Thanks for applying to Google!
emails.body:
Hi Karthik Sreedhar,

Thanks for applying to Google! There are a ton of great companies out  
there, so
we appreciate your interest in joining our team.

We’ll review your application carefully and keep
Kavya Sreedhar, Abhijit Adsule updated on your progress. In the
meantime, learn more about our job opportunities and life at Google on our

- The Google Recruiting Team

emails.subject: Thanks for accepting Kavya Sreedhar’s invitation to apply to Google
emails.body:
Google Careers

Time to find the perfect role at Google

Hi Karthik Sreedhar,

We're thrilled that you've accepted Kavya's invitation to apply to Google!  
You have 30 days to apply to up to 3 jobs with this invitation, which also  
enables Kavya to receive updates on the status of your application(s). Make  
sure to apply using the invitation link and the same email address where  
you received the invitation. If you already applied within the last 30 days  
you don't have to apply again. However, your referrer may not receive  
updates on those applications. Remember, if you want to apply to additional  
roles once this invitation has expired, be sure to ask Kavya to send you a  
new invitation.

EXPLORE OPEN ROLES

Looking forward to seeing your application!

-The Google Staffing team

Info icon Need help finding the right role?

Kavya can always give you some pointers, but generally we recommend  
focusing first on roles where your skills match the minimum qualifications  
and some of the prefered qualifications, then tailoring your resume to  
highlight those skills. Learn more about how Google hires.

This email was sent to you as a transactional notification of activity  
related to Google Staffing.

Google logo
1600 Amphitheatre Parkway,
Mountain View, CA 94043, USA

You received this message because someone that works at Google referred you.

emails.subject: Invitation from Abhijit Adsule to apply to Google
emails.body:
Google Careers logo

Build for everyone

Abhijit wants to work with you at Google

Hi KARTHIK SREEDHAR,

Abhijit has invited you to apply for a job at Google! Start the application  
process by viewing their invitation. By accepting their invitation, you'll  
allow us to share high level application status updates with this Googler.  
They will see the next 1-3 roles you apply to in the coming 30 days, as  
well as any roles you've applied to in the past 30 days where the  
application is still open. If you'd like Abhijit to be kept in the loop, be  
sure to accept and apply through the link using your ks4190@columbia.edu  
email address! If you want to later apply to more roles, make sure to ask  
them to send you a new invitation. You may also decline the invitation if  
you'd prefer that status updates not be shared with Abhijit.

The link to accept is only active for the next 30 days, so accept it soon.  
Then, follow the “Search jobs” link to learn more about open Google roles  
and apply. If you've applied to roles in the last 30 days, you don't have  
to apply again.

Make sure to use this invitation link so we know you've been referred.

VIEW INVITATION TO APPLY

We hope to hear from you soon,

The Google Staffing Team

Please note, any information you enter during the application and  
recruitment process will be kept confidential in compliance with Google's  
Candidate Privacy Policy.

Google logo

© 2021 Google LLC 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

You received this message because someone that works at Google referred you.

email address! If you want to later apply to more roles, make sure to ask  
them to send you a new invitation. You may also decline the invitation if  
you'd prefer that status updates not be shared with Abhijit.

The link to accept is only active for the next 30 days, so accept it soon.  
Then, follow the “Search jobs” link to learn more about open Google roles  
and apply. If you've applied to roles in the last 30 days, you don't have  
to apply again.

Make sure to use this invitation link so we know you've been referred.

VIEW INVITATION TO APPLY

We hope to hear from you soon,

The Google Staffing Team

Please note, any information you enter during the application and  
recruitment process will be kept confidential in compliance with Google's  
Candidate Privacy Policy.

Google logo

Dear Computer Scientist,

Mercor Intelligence is hiring for a variety of projects to start this
winter and in 2026. We have part-time remote positions available in
computer science, AI, and data science. Our AI company assists major
artificial intelligence and social media companies in San Francisco and
Silicon Valley. We recruit computer scientists around the nation and around
the world. To learn more about our positions available now, click the links
below.

Part-time or Full-time, Remote

emails.body:
can you come to my office?

On Thu, Oct 23, 2025 at 6:09 PM Karthik Sreedhar <ks4190@columbia.edu>
wrote:

> If you still have the terminal open, any chance you could send the logs
> from the Load More step? I have print statements there to flag when an
> email already in the database is skipped and it seems to be working on my
> end so trying to figure out what might have went wrong. But see the issue
> with loading multiple emails from the same thread, have fixed that!
>
> On Oct 23, 2025, at 3:53 PM, Lydia Chilton <chilton@cs.columbia.edu>
> wrote:
>
> ﻿
> awesome :) I was just gonna say to some say hi!
>
> On Thu, Oct 23, 2025 at 3:52 PM Karthik Sreedhar <ks4190@columbia.edu>
> wrote:
>
>> Actually I'll just come by, I think I got it. Hopefully no new errors but
>> trying my best to go through everything now !!
>>
>> On Thu, Oct 23, 2025 at 3:29 PM Karthik Sreedhar <ks4190@columbia.edu>
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Lydia,
>>>
>>>
>>> I’ve fixed the pure bugs we ran into yesterday and I think I fixed the
>>> Load More Other categorization — but I’m still working on the functionality
>>> to default suggest the sender as the category when seeding (that part alone
>>> is easy, but I’m working on making sure renaming that category persists
>>> amongst the other suggestions as well which has been annoying and taking
>>> some time). I also probably want to stress test the system a more to make
>>> sure we don’t run into any other new silly bugs.
>>>
>>>
>>> I’m on campus so I can stop by at 4:30 still, and hopefully probably
>>> will make more progress in the next hour — but given we have two meetings
>>> tomorrow (or even if just one if the morning one is cancelled?) I was
>>> wondering if it would make more sense for me to continue ironing out these
>>> changes and just talk tomorrow instead.
>>>
>>>
>>> But let me know, I can still come by if at least to say hi.
>>>
>>>
>>> Thanks,
>>>
>>> Karthik
>>>
>>>

emails.subject: RE: Welcome to the Columbia University Student Health Insurance Plan
emails.body:
Hi Karthik,

Thank you for your email and I hope you are doing well!

All full-time students are automatically enrolled unless they manually request to opt out and are approved for a waiver. Students need to submit a new waiver request annually for each plan year. The Fall enrollment period ran from July 15 –September 30, 2025.

If you have existing insurance coverage which meets the waiver criteria<https://www.health.columbia.edu/content/request-waiver > and missed the deadline, you may submit a Late Waiver Request. Please upload the following documents through your Patient Portal<https://cas.columbia.edu/cas/login?service=https%3a%2f%2fsecure.health.columbia.edu > for review:

  1.  The Request for Late Waiver Form – found in your Patient Portal<https://cas.columbia.edu/cas/login?service=https%3a%2f%2fsecure.health.columbia.edu > under Downloadable Forms on the left-hand side menu.

  1.  A copy of the front and back of your insurance card.

  1.  A Proof of Coverage Letter from your existing insurance company that states your name and the effective date of coverage. We are unable to accept email notices.

Once received, please allow typically up to 7-10 business days for processing. If approved, your Columbia insurance will be terminated as of the date of submission and your student account will be pro-rated and adjusted accordingly.

Warm Regards,

Giselle Roman

Health Insurance Representative
Columbia University Health
https://www.health.columbia.edu/ 

Confidentiality Disclaimer: This e-mail message and any attachments are private communication and may contain confidential, privileged information meant solely for the intended recipient. If you are not the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that any use, dissemination, distribution or copying of this communication is strictly prohibited. Please notify the sender immediately by replying to this message, then delete the e-mail and any attachments from your system. Thank you.

From: Karthik Sreedhar <ks4190@columbia.edu>
Sent: Thursday, October 23, 2025 3:19 PM

If you are residing more than 50 miles away from campus referrals are not required.

emails.snippet: Hi Karthik, Thank you for your email and I hope you are doing well! All full-time students are automatically enrolled unless they manually request to opt out and are approved for a waiver. Students
emails.originalBody:
Hi Karthik,

Thank you for your email and I hope you are doing well!

All full-time students are automatically enrolled unless they manually request to opt out and are approved for a waiver. Students need to submit a new waiver request annually for each plan year. The Fall enrollment period ran from July 15 –September 30, 2025.

If you have existing insurance coverage which meets the waiver criteria<https://www.health.columbia.edu/content/request-waiver > and missed the deadline, you may submit a Late Waiver Request. Please upload the following documents through your Patient Portal<https://cas.columbia.edu/cas/login?service=https%3a%2f%2fsecure.health.columbia.edu > for review:

  1.  The Request for Late Waiver Form – found in your Patient Portal<https://cas.columbia.edu/cas/login?service=https%3a%2f%2fsecure.health.columbia.edu > under Downloadable Forms on the left-hand side menu.

  1.  A copy of the front and back of your insurance card.

  1.  A Proof of Coverage Letter from your existing insurance company that states your name and the effective date of coverage. We are unable to accept email notices.

Once received, please allow typically up to 7-10 business days for processing. If approved, your Columbia insurance will be terminated as of the date of submission and your student account will be pro-rated and adjusted accordingly.

Warm Regards,

Giselle Roman

Health Insurance Representative
Columbia University Health
https://www.health.columbia.edu/

If you are not the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that any use, dissemination, distribution or copying of this communication is strictly prohibited. Please notify the sender immediately by replying to this message, then delete the e-mail and any attachments from your system. Thank you.

emails.subject: PhD Medical Campus Mixer!
emails.body:
Hi PhD students!

Gigi here, the Engineering Graduate Student Council (EGSC) Interschool
Chair. You may or may not have been to my EGSC Cocoahosue events, or more
recently, Pitch Night with the Columbia Business School. My role in this
position is to integrate different schools/campuses for events. Currently,
I am a biomedical engineering PhD student working on the medical campus,
and have heard that many of you who work up there would like more
programming nearby. That being said, I am very excited to announce that *EGSC
is hosting our first-ever PhD mixer on the uptown medical campus! 💃*

We will be hanging out at *Fort Washington (3938 Broadway) *from
*5:30-7:30PM* with our fellow colleagues from the Graduate Student
Organization (GSO), which is the body that governs the VP&S Biomedical
Sciences PhD students (think neurology, nutrition, microbiology, the list
goes on!).

This event is intended for* PhD students who work on the medical
campus*. Eventbrite
tickets are accessible here
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.eventbrite.com/e/phd-uptown-mixer-tickets-1815621726459?aff=oddtdtcreator__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!W_OnMbBEAu1ibBGL8heEoMn_JXk27Oh6YmWkwIbJX6rF56eTUOp7d8gK3NsrWLr4eGd7UUknpcv4bw-fR1ngdp7GjNCMY6CRKzNei-a_1US5CjJSLcnSuRk$ >.
Food and drinks will be provided.

Hope to see you there!

-- 
____________________________________________________________

*Gigi Bond*
Inter-school Chair, Engineering Graduate Student Council
*Columbia Engineering*
interschool-chair@egsc.seas.columbia.edu

emails.subject: Cocoahouse this Thursday ☕️🍪| EGSC Weekly Newsletter
emails.body:
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/  >
*Hey Grad SEAS!*
Happy Tuesday! We hope you had a great time at our *October Carleton
Commons Social* last Friday 🍕🪩🕺💃!

This *Thursday*, we're hosting our *EGSC Cocoahouse*! See you there!

Don't forget to *follow us on Instagram @columbiaegsc
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.instagram.com/columbiaegsc/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!SVb6M51tAEHXSOqSq5Ut6fAuy5Ql_-GyB4z5jWOV4YxJbGk3BCb3D6bZx0YytbmBerdG5A6e296h6hqw3mmLU7BODytRV1bd04u-sHt54pyT0UkFV38$ > *to stay updated on all our
events!
*Caffeinate and conversate* with other students from graduate SEAS! Enjoy
coffee, cookies, and some great company *this Thursday* at *EGSC Cocoa
House*!
Join us at our *weekly GBMs*, whether you are a Department Representative
or not! We meet on Fridays from 4-5 pm in Mudd 524.

This week, we will be joined by *Stan Sakry, Senior Director of Facilities
and Space Planning*!

*Lunch & Learn: LGBTQ+ History Month Edition*
Come grab lunch and celebrate LGBTQ+ History Month with us! While you eat,
enjoy a fun slideshow of surprising facts, trailblazing figures, and
milestones from LGBTQ+ history!
Interested in joining *Columbia’s only student club dedicated to
Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI)*? At *Neurotech X Columbia*, you’ll find
opportunities to engage in cutting-edge research projects  and connect with
peers passionate about neurotechnology. We welcome all members of the
Columbia community to join us — please register through this Google Form:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/1r6jLNyCo6pbxXR3jEqPT0a_FzcpqlB4d4iCH3_75ZbQ__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!SVb6M51tAEHXSOqSq5Ut6fAuy5Ql_-GyB4z5jWOV4YxJbGk3BCb3D6bZx0YytbmBerdG5A6e296h6hqw3mmLU7BODytRV1bd04u-sHt54pyTV4bt_Qw$ 
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/1r6jLNyCo6pbxXR3jEqPT0a_FzcpqlB4d4iCH3_75ZbQ__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!U66AXcO3CK09cjy5CEinktcs0lyktt1ow3JxvVucDXBZcW_LeYTqdaFHhq0nJeCzhWoF8A1KI_e8L7oaewiVfhUko8lKWg$>
RSVP Here!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeJDkw_2wSSNtV-ajNrS97xSEPgzuBhy2XvVXUlVSJS1dpnGg/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!SVb6M51tAEHXSOqSq5Ut6fAuy5Ql_-GyB4z5jWOV4YxJbGk3BCb3D6bZx0YytbmBerdG5A6e296h6hqw3mmLU7BODytRV1bd04u-sHt54pyTCel1tCA$ >
[image: Instagram] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://instagram.com/columbiaegsc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!SVb6M51tAEHXSOqSq5Ut6fAuy5Ql_-GyB4z5jWOV4YxJbGk3BCb3D6bZx0YytbmBerdG5A6e296h6hqw3mmLU7BODytRV1bd04u-sHt54pyTanVxhrw$ >
[image: LinkedIn] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/company/egsccolumbia__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!SVb6M51tAEHXSOqSq5Ut6fAuy5Ql_-GyB4z5jWOV4YxJbGk3BCb3D6bZx0YytbmBerdG5A6e296h6hqw3mmLU7BODytRV1bd04u-sHt54pyTapG8sqc$ >
[image: Website] <https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/  >
[image: Email] <communications-chair@egsc.seas.columbia.edu>
*Copyright © 2025 EGSC Columbia University, All rights reserved.*
You are receiving this email because you are a graduate student at CU SEAS.

*Our mailing address is:*
EGSC Columbia University
500 W 120th St
New York, NY 10027-6623

Tuesday, October 21, 2025 • 3 min read
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Wake up with The Yodel
 |

| 
by Natasha Frost with Anthony Rivas
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Wake up with The Yodel
 |

| 
by Natasha Frost with Anthony Rivas
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
Good morning, folks. The night sky will host a spectacular display tonight, with the Orionid meteor show coinciding with a new moon. Here’s how to see it. Now, on to the news.
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Need to know
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

| 
Kiichiro Sato/AP
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
A massive outage of Amazon’s cloud computing service disrupted almost every corner of the internet yesterday, including our ability to send this newsletter. Services were back to normal last night.
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|    
   - Background: Companies rely on cloud computing providers like Amazon to store their data and make software and applications run via data plants across the U.S., including one in Virginia, where the Domain Name System error occurred. [CBS News/Business Insider]   

   - Impacts: The outage prevented students from accessing their work, hungry diners from using delivery apps and crypto traders from using exchanges. It also disrupted Amazon-owned smart speakers, doorbell cameras and e-books. If it messed up your day too, here’s how to manage the next one. [AP]
   - Experts weigh in: The outage laid bare just how many global businesses rely on a small number of tech companies, analysts said. “We urgently need diversification in cloud computing,” one digital rights expert said. [The Guardian]
 |

| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
In case you missed it
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

| 
Daniel Shirey/MLB Photos via Getty Images
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
Toronto triumphs in ALCS
 |

| 
In last night’s tiebreaking Game 7, the Blue Jays soared to a 4-3 win over the Mariners, thanks to a towering three-run homer by George Springer. Next, they’ll face the Dodgers in their first World Series appearance in 32 years, beginning Friday. Here’s what to expect. [Yahoo Sports]
 |

| 
Natl. Guard in Portland
 |

| 
An appellate court ruled that the Trump administration can deploy the National Guard in Portland, Ore., though another court order still blocks immediate action. Oregon officials vowed to appeal. Read the latest on President Trump’s efforts to federalize troops. [CNN]
 |

| 
Trump’s White House ballroom
 |

| 
Construction crews began tearing down part of the White House’s East Wing to make way for Trump’s $250 million ballroom, despite lacking federal approval. Trump previously said it was “privately funded” by “generous Patriots.” See pictures of the demolition. [AP]
 |

| 
A landmark blindness study
 |

| 
A small clinical study found that eight in 10 patients with severe macular degeneration experienced meaningful improvement in their vision after receiving a wireless eye implant and pairing it with augmented reality glasses — though it was only in black and white. [NBC News]
 |

| 
‘See you in Oz!’
 |

| 
Oscar nominee Colman Domingo will voice the grown-up Cowardly Lion in Wicked: For Good, after director Jon M. Chu reached out via Instagram DM. The sequel comes out Nov. 21. Watch the reveal, and see who’s part of the returning cast. [Yahoo Entertainment]
 |

| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
What’s happening today
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

| 
James Manning/PA Images via Getty Images
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
In bookstores
 |

| 
Nobody’s Girl: A Memoir of Surviving Abuse and Fighting for Justice, the posthumous memoir by Virginia Giuffre, written with Amy Wallace, is out. The book details the alleged abuses by disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein and Prince Andrew. Read a preview. [USA Today]
 |

| 
Now streaming
 |

| 
This one’s for baseball fans (Canadians especially): Who Killed the Montreal Expos?, which investigates who is to blame for the downfall of Canada’s first MLB team, is on Netflix. [Hollywood Reporter]
 |

| 
On the court
 |

| 
The 2025-26 NBA season tips off on NBC, with a 7:30 p.m. ET matchup between the Rockets and the Thunder, followed by a Warriors-Lakers faceoff at 10 p.m. ET. Here’s your guide to the full season. [AP/Yahoo Sports]
 |

| 
On TV
 |

| 
Wife Swap: The Real Housewives Edition premieres at 9 p.m. ET on Bravo. On tonight’s episode, Salt Lake City’s Angie Katsanevas slips out of her stilettos and into the life of former Mormon Lindsay Flake. [Us Weekly]
 |

| 
And don't forget to...
 |

| 
Read your daily horoscope. Play the crossword. Check the forecast in your area.
 |

| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Today in history
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
In 1879, Thomas Edison invented the light bulb. Or, did he? The road to electric light was twistier than you might’ve heard. Although Edison and his team were the first to successfully test an incandescent light bulb, which burned for more than 13 hours, they built on the work of many others. Here’s how. [BGR]
 |

| 
Illustration: Yahoo News; photo: Bettmann Archive via Getty Images
 |

| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Feel-good moment
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

| 
Kelli Bender
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
The 35th annual Halloween dog parade was held Sunday in New York City’s Tompkins Square Park — in my opinion, one of the city’s best days of the year. Check out some of the best canine costumes. I like the Alien one. [People]
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Your daily advice
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
There’s a simple way to make pasta leftovers taste almost as good as when the dish was fresh. Just save a little pasta water, then take these steps. [The Kitchn]
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
The Yodel team
 |

|

|  |

| 
Tuesday, October 21, 2025 • 3 min read
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Wake up with The Yodel
 |

| 
by Natasha Frost with Anthony Rivas
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Wake up with The Yodel
 |

| 
by Natasha Frost with Anthony Rivas
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
Good morning, folks. The night sky will host a spectacular display tonight, with the Orionid meteor show coinciding with a new moon. Here’s how to see it. Now, on to the news.
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Need to know
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

| 
Kiichiro Sato/AP
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
A massive outage of Amazon’s cloud computing service disrupted almost every corner of the internet yesterday, including our ability to send this newsletter. Services were back to normal last night.
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|    
   - Background: Companies rely on cloud computing providers like Amazon to store their data and make software and applications run via data plants across the U.S., including one in Virginia, where the Domain Name System error occurred. [CBS News/Business Insider]   

   - Impacts: The outage prevented students from accessing their work, hungry diners from using delivery apps and crypto traders from using exchanges. It also disrupted Amazon-owned smart speakers, doorbell cameras and e-books. If it messed up your day too, here’s how to manage the next one. [AP]
   - Experts weigh in: The outage laid bare just how many global businesses rely on a small number of tech companies, analysts said. “We urgently need diversification in cloud computing,” one digital rights expert said. [The Guardian]
 |

| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
In case you missed it
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

| 
Daniel Shirey/MLB Photos via Getty Images
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
Toronto triumphs in ALCS
 |

| 
In last night’s tiebreaking Game 7, the Blue Jays soared to a 4-3 win over the Mariners, thanks to a towering three-run homer by George Springer. Next, they’ll face the Dodgers in their first World Series appearance in 32 years, beginning Friday. Here’s what to expect. [Yahoo Sports]
 |

| 
Natl. Guard in Portland
 |

| 
An appellate court ruled that the Trump administration can deploy the National Guard in Portland, Ore., though another court order still blocks immediate action. Oregon officials vowed to appeal. Read the latest on President Trump’s efforts to federalize troops. [CNN]
 |

| 
Trump’s White House ballroom
 |

| 
Construction crews began tearing down part of the White House’s East Wing to make way for Trump’s $250 million ballroom, despite lacking federal approval. Trump previously said it was “privately funded” by “generous Patriots.” See pictures of the demolition. [AP]
 |

| 
A landmark blindness study
 |

| 
A small clinical study found that eight in 10 patients with severe macular degeneration experienced meaningful improvement in their vision after receiving a wireless eye implant and pairing it with augmented reality glasses — though it was only in black and white. [NBC News]
 |

| 
‘See you in Oz!’
 |

| 
Oscar nominee Colman Domingo will voice the grown-up Cowardly Lion in Wicked: For Good, after director Jon M. Chu reached out via Instagram DM. The sequel comes out Nov. 21. Watch the reveal, and see who’s part of the returning cast. [Yahoo Entertainment]
 |

| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
What’s happening today
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

| 
James Manning/PA Images via Getty Images
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
In bookstores
 |

| 
Nobody’s Girl: A Memoir of Surviving Abuse and Fighting for Justice, the posthumous memoir by Virginia Giuffre, written with Amy Wallace, is out. The book details the alleged abuses by disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein and Prince Andrew. Read a preview. [USA Today]
 |

| 
Now streaming
 |

| 
This one’s for baseball fans (Canadians especially): Who Killed the Montreal Expos?, which investigates who is to blame for the downfall of Canada’s first MLB team, is on Netflix. [Hollywood Reporter]
 |

| 
On the court
 |

| 
The 2025-26 NBA season tips off on NBC, with a 7:30 p.m. ET matchup between the Rockets and the Thunder, followed by a Warriors-Lakers faceoff at 10 p.m. ET. Here’s your guide to the full season. [AP/Yahoo Sports]
 |

| 
On TV
 |

| 
Wife Swap: The Real Housewives Edition premieres at 9 p.m. ET on Bravo. On tonight’s episode, Salt Lake City’s Angie Katsanevas slips out of her stilettos and into the life of former Mormon Lindsay Flake. [Us Weekly]
 |

| 
And don't forget to...
 |

| 
Read your daily horoscope. Play the crossword. Check the forecast in your area.
 |

| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Today in history
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
In 1879, Thomas Edison invented the light bulb. Or, did he? The road to electric light was twistier than you might’ve heard. Although Edison and his team were the first to successfully test an incandescent light bulb, which burned for more than 13 hours, they built on the work of many others. Here’s how. [BGR]
 |

| 
Illustration: Yahoo News; photo: Bettmann Archive via Getty Images
 |

| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Feel-good moment
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

| 
Kelli Bender
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
The 35th annual Halloween dog parade was held Sunday in New York City’s Tompkins Square Park — in my opinion, one of the city’s best days of the year. Check out some of the best canine costumes. I like the Alien one. [People]
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Your daily advice
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
There’s a simple way to make pasta leftovers taste almost as good as when the dish was fresh. Just save a little pasta water, then take these steps. [The Kitchn]
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
The Yodel team
 |

|

emails.subject: ✨ Welcome to New York's biggest AI events calendar
emails.body:
✨ Welcome to New York's biggest AI events calendar
Hi there, Thank you for joining us at one of our co-hosted events! I'm happy to welcome you to the largest weekly newsletter dedicated to AI events GenerativeAINY.com At any given moment, almost 50 in-person AI events, from hackathons to conferences, workshops, and meetups, are scheduled in New York alone. Being on the list means you’ll receive updates on these events and other carefully curated AI gatherings across NY every Thursday at 7.30am ET. This calendar is not company, group, or host-specific, making it the most comprehensive calendar with AI events only. [ ✨ New] Join our growing communities: Become a Premium member to join a network of top-tier AI operators and founders and get free tickets to top conferences. Join the AI Judging community to judge world-class hackathons and strengthen your O-1/EB-1 visa petition. Partner with me to grow the biggest AI events community across the Bay Area & NY (can become a paid gig). Join a community - https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/genai-sf__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QoyO0__Fwpzq2lIg5BXsI_XKqkPoEpRDtBetXBDHFiFULbwXE0uBwThxUFC_hAl26luN3dCXOAsa3fnyqT0jtKViXm8ukYe1$  Not interested in these updates? No problem, simply click on the unsubscribe link below and you won't be contacted again. Otherwise, reply to this email with "Hey!" so the spam filter gods will spare future emails. Want to feature or highlight your event to 10k AI professionals? Sponsor an event? Reply to this email. Looking forward to seeing you at future events! Best, Sahar P.S. Are you Bay Area-bound or travel often? Check out the SF AI events calendar GenerativeAISF.com
