emails.subject: Your monthly Columbia University statement
emails.body:
Your monthly Columbia University statement

Student ID: C004741301

This is your monthly billing notification.  Your action may be needed.  You will have access to monthly statements for the remainder of the year through Student Services Online (SSOL).

Dear Karthik Sreedhar:

The monthly statement for your Columbia University account is now available on SSOL and will show an anticipated amount due or (credit) of $26.00.  Note: if the amount is in parentheses, it is an anticipated credit.If the amount shown is zero, no action is needed. If the amount shown is greater than zero make your payment by or before 11/06/2025. Balances that remain unpaid at the due date may incur late fees and will prevent students from registering, changing programs, or obtaining a diploma. We recognize these are extremely challenging and unique times. If you are a student experiencing financial hardship or are unable to register for an upcoming term as a result of account balances and need help financing your balance , please contact your school's financial aid office for assistance.Make your payment at SSOL at https://ssol.columbia.edu/ .  Please note: as of November 1, 2016, the University no longer accepts cash payments for student accounts. If you have arranged to receive funding from your academic department, that funding may not appear in your student account until the early registration period for the following term. If your funding does not appear by then, inquire with your department. If the amount shown is an anticipated credit -- indicated in parentheses as ($xx,xxx.xx) -- it may be the result of a financial aid award, a tuition deposit or overpayment of charges to your account. 

Please note: The amount shown is a snapshot of your account on the day the statement was produced.  This amount may not reflect any recent changes in your registration.  After you complete your registration, please review the recent activity in SSOL to see your most current amount due.

To avoid any impact to your registration, we encourage you to review your account regularly in SSOL. emails.originalBody:
Your monthly Columbia University statement

Student ID: C004741301

This is your monthly billing notification.  Your action may be needed.  You will have access to monthly statements for the remainder of the year through Student Services Online (SSOL).

Dear Karthik Sreedhar:

The monthly statement for your Columbia University account is now available on SSOL and will show an anticipated amount due or (credit) of $26.00.  Note: if the amount is in parentheses, it is an anticipated credit.If the amount shown is zero, no action is needed. If the amount shown is greater than zero make your payment by or before 11/06/2025. Balances that remain unpaid at the due date may incur late fees and will prevent students from registering, changing programs, or obtaining a diploma. We recognize these are extremely challenging and unique times. If you are a student experiencing financial hardship or are unable to register for an upcoming term as a result of account balances and need help financing your balance , please contact your school's financial aid office for assistance.Make your payment at SSOL at https://ssol.columbia.edu/ .  Please note: as of November 1, 2016, the University no longer accepts cash payments for student accounts. If you have arranged to receive funding from your academic department, that funding may not appear in your student account until the early registration period for the following term. If your funding does not appear by then, inquire with your department. If the amount shown is an anticipated credit -- indicated in parentheses as ($xx,xxx.xx) -- it may be the result of a financial aid award, a tuition deposit or overpayment of charges to your account. 

Please note: The amount shown is a snapshot of your account on the day the statement was produced.  This amount may not reflect any recent changes in your registration.  After you complete your registration, please review the recent activity in SSOL to see your most current amount due.

no worries - I did bump it to 2:30, but zoom is good too :) Or we can catch
up tomorrow.

wrote:

> Hi,
>

>

> of there moving forward.
>
> Sincerely apologize, I thought an 11:30 appointment would be fine to make
> it by 2 but forgot to consider how badly behind radiology runs :(
>
> Thanks,
> Karthik
>

emails.subject: Invitation from Kavya Sreedhar to apply to Google
emails.body:
Google Careers logo

Build for everyone

Kavya wants to work with you at Google

Hi Karthik Sreedhar,

Kavya has invited you to apply for a job at Google! Start the application  
process by viewing their invitation. By accepting their invitation, you'll  
allow us to share high level application status updates with this Googler.  
They will see the next 1-3 roles you apply to in the coming 30 days, as  
well as any roles you've applied to in the past 30 days where the  
application is still open. If you'd like Kavya to be kept in the loop, be  
sure to accept and apply through the link using your ks4190@columbia.edu  
email address! If you want to later apply to more roles, make sure to ask  
them to send you a new invitation. You may also decline the invitation if  
you'd prefer that status updates not be shared with Kavya.

The link to accept is only active for the next 30 days, so accept it soon.  
Then, follow the “Search jobs” link to learn more about open Google roles  
and apply. If you've applied to roles in the last 30 days, you don't have  
to apply again.

Make sure to use this invitation link so we know you've been referred.

VIEW INVITATION TO APPLY

We hope to hear from you soon,

The Google Staffing Team

Please note, any information you enter during the application and  
recruitment process will be kept confidential in compliance with Google's  
Candidate Privacy Policy.

Google logo

© 2021 Google LLC 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

You received this message because someone that works at Google referred you.

emails.subject: Study Sign-Up Confirmation
emails.originalFrom: Columbia Business School Behavioral Research Lab <columbia-brl-admin@sona-systems.net>
emails.category: Columbia Business School Behavioral Research Lab
categories: Columbia Business School Behavioral Research Lab
emails.body:
This email is to notify you that you have been signed up for the following online study:

Study Name: New York Runner Survey
Participation Deadline: Monday, October 20, 2025 8:00 PM
Website: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia-brl.sona-systems.com/webstudy_access.aspx?p_experiment_id=1178&p_person_id=50877&p_signup_id=26D92&p_checksum=9F0D4FCFC7731C63D2EB9239C1CEC423__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!TD1LRkF6K9F85rhS4w75T1pZbYIR0r51DkXdMUMKis3kLX8SZtMHdH9sYJ9NE9zZ2pG0PMqYW0f9gkrEjifJPHH_lA4Ol00uUBXaNw$ 
Researcher: Mike White <mwhite25@gsb.columbia.edu>

You may cancel your sign-up until Monday, October 20, 2025 7:00 PM.

If you have any further questions, you may contact the researcher or logon to the site for more information: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia-brl.sona-systems.com__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!TD1LRkF6K9F85rhS4w75T1pZbYIR0r51DkXdMUMKis3kLX8SZtMHdH9sYJ9NE9zZ2pG0PMqYW0f9gkrEjifJPHH_lA4Ol00C8ExJag$ 

NOTE: You are only identified to researchers by your Identity Code so be sure to provide them with this code when you attend the study: 155638

Thank you for signing up to participate in research in the Behavioral Research Lab. Please arrive to the study on time. You will not be allowed to participate if you are more than 5 minutes late. 

In the event of a last minute emergency that prevents you from attending your time slot, and you're unable to cancel your time slot on SONA, please email the researchers on the study and inform them that you will not be able to attend. 

Directions can be found here:
http://www8.gsb.columbia.edu/behaviorlab/directions 

Video directions: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://youtu.be/mcjJ_zddhTc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!TD1LRkF6K9F85rhS4w75T1pZbYIR0r51DkXdMUMKis3kLX8SZtMHdH9sYJ9NE9zZ2pG0PMqYW0f9gkrEjifJPHH_lA4Ol03CUzNDFg$ 

Please take note of the following lab locations:

Rooms B203, B204, and B205: Located in the BASEMENT of Uris Hall, through an exterior door outside of the Deli (under the stairs). They cannot be accessed from inside Uris and are NOT LOCATED on the second floor of Uris Hall. 

Eligibility Requirements: New York City Resident; Runner
Brief Description: We are surveying how runners in NYC feel in real-time. Open the survey right before a run. Read the instructions until you reach the STOP page. Then, go on your run. After you finish, return to the survey to answer a few follow-ups about how you felt.

Detailed Description: 
We are surveying how runners in NYC feel in real-time. You will open the survey right before a run. Read the instructions until you reach the STOP page. Then, go on your run. After you finish, return to the survey to answer a few follow-ups about how you felt.

XDRABS-TDBRAEE-SEGAWRQ-OWGSEDGWDSFAD-EWGSEDGWDBSBA-PBADQBR-MDBDRQ

emails.subject: SEAS Midterm Evaluations Reminder
emails.body:
Dear Karthik Sreedhar, The Engineering School values your thoughtful feedback on your courses. Please take a few minutes to fill out the Course evaluations. These evaluations are extremely important for several reasons: • They help the Engineering
ZjQcmQRYFpfptBannerStart
This Message Is From an External Sender
This message came from outside your organization.
 
ZjQcmQRYFpfptBannerEnd
Dear Karthik Sreedhar,

The Engineering School values your thoughtful feedback on your courses. Please take a few minutes to fill out the Course evaluations. These evaluations are extremely important for several reasons:

• They help the Engineering School and Departments evaluate instructor-effectiveness
• Instructors use the feedback to improve their courses for future students
• Your feedback helps fellow students choose their courses

The evaluation is available at:




Survey Summary

Course CodeCourse TitleSurvey Start DateSurvey End DateStatus
COMSE9902_048_2025_3-SEMINARINCOMPUTERSCIENCECOMSE9902_048_2025_3 - SEMINAR IN COMPUTER SCIENCE10/13/2025 12:00 AM10/20/2025 11:59 PMNot-SubmittedGo To Survey



This evaluation will remain open until 10/20/2025 11:59 PM. Please complete it before then.

Enter the site using your username and password. You may submit the evaluation once only.
If the above link is not working then please follow the Alternate Instructions at the bottom of the message.

Thank you for your time and attention in this important matter. Your feedback is very important.

Please note: Evaluations are confidential. Your instructors and TA's will never see your name associated with any evaluation responses.
------------------------------------------------------------
Should you encounter any technical difficulty in filling out the evaluation, please send an email to seas-learningsupport@columbia.edu clearly indicating the problem you encountered. For any other concerns please contact your department.

Alternate Instructions:
1) Go to Login (Note: This link should not be shared with others; it is unique to you.)
2) Enter your username and password and click on 'Login' button.
3) Go to your course
4) Click on 'Evaluation' link in the left navigation menu.

emails.subject: discussing progress and POSTERs this week at lunch
emails.body:
Hi All - this week during the DAPLab lunch we'll chat about our recent
research updates and discuss what to put on posters.

Posters should be 24" x 36". We'd like to have about 20 posters. It's a
great way to get jobs/internships/investment!

See you Wednesday!
lydia

emails.body:
Dear Participants, 

We are excited to let you know that new timeslots are now available for several studies. If you are interested in participating, please log in to your account to view the studies and reserve a time that works for you. 

--- 

Velmara Study – In-Person (Uris Hall BRL)
 Participate in a quick 7-min study. After completing a simple task, fill out a short Qualtrics survey and you will receive a $5 Amazon gift card. 

Sign up here: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia-brl.sona-systems.com/default.aspx?p_return_experiment_id=1171__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!VRu0yMTikCLthfORjvrPLjCxZrPsLOzx0rFSkyftEbAfiaR0s4-rTaW7KTE-5eEXqIl_jVrBVCuQFqGwKLAAIEahncevSbrWlpd6KQ$   

--- 

New York Runner Survey – Online
 The study is surveying how runners in NYC feel in real-time. Open the survey right before a run. Read the instructions until you reach the STOP page. Then, go on your run. After you finish, return to the survey to answer a few follow-ups about how you felt. The study takes 3 minutes and you will receive $5 Amazon gift card. 

Sign up here: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia-brl.sona-systems.com/default.aspx?p_return_experiment_id=1178__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!VRu0yMTikCLthfORjvrPLjCxZrPsLOzx0rFSkyftEbAfiaR0s4-rTaW7KTE-5eEXqIl_jVrBVCuQFqGwKLAAIEahncevSbpQFv1JeQ$  

--- 

Thank you for your continued participation. We greatly appreciate your support! 

Best, 

The BRL Team

emails.subject: CCPE - 2026 Graduate Student Grant Opportunity
emails.body:
Dear SEAS Doctoral Students,

The *Columbia Center for Political Economy (CCPE)*
<http://politicaleconomy.columbia.edu/  > is now accepting applications for
its *2026 Graduate Student Grant Opportunity*.

*About the Grant*
These one-year awards support doctoral research that advances new knowledge
and fosters collaboration in the broad field of political economy. Research
areas include:

   - Firms and Industrial Policy
   - Work and Labor
   - Money and Finance
   - Climate
   - International Political Economy

*Funding*

   - Awards range from *$5,000–$15,000*
   - Larger awards may support collaborative or cross-departmental projects
   - In exceptional cases (with departmental approval), a semester-long
   teaching buyout may be funded

*Eligibility*
All *Columbia doctoral students* are eligible to apply. Cross-departmental
proposals are welcome.

*Deadline:* Monday, *October 20, 2025, at 5:00 p.m. ET*
*Award period:* January 1–December 31, 2026

*Apply Here!
<https://politicaleconomy.columbia.edu/news/now-open-2026-graduate-student-grant-opportunity  >*

Best,

*Graduate Student Affairs*

*Columbia Engineering*

emails.subject: REMINDER: Apply for February 11, 2026 MS Degree Conferral by November 1, 2025
emails.body:
Dear students,

If you are completing your MS degree requirements by the end of this Fall
semester, you should apply for the February 11, 2026 degree conferral. (
This includes MS-PhD students completing the MS portion of their program.)

The deadline to submit the degree application for the February 11, 2026 degree
conferral is Saturday, November 1, 2025. You must submit the University
Application for Degree form via SSOL
<https://registrar.columbia.edu/content/graduation-requirements-and-diplomas  >
by
this deadline in order to be considered for degree conferral. After the
Registrar’s Office processes your degree application, Engineering Student
Affairs and your academic department will assess if you have met all degree
requirements.

Note that the review of student records for degree conferral is not
automatic and only occurs if the student submits the degree
application. Students
who fail to complete the degree application by the deadline will NOT be
reviewed for degree conferral, and the degree will NOT be conferred on February
11, 2026. In such situations, the next possible degree conferral would be
in May 2026.

If you have concerns about meeting degree requirements, please reach out to
your academic advisor as soon as possible.

Sincerely,

SEAS Academics

Engineering Student Affairs

emails.subject: PAST DUE REQUIREMENT: SEAS Mandatory Orientation Tutorials
emails.body:
Dear student,

We are reaching out because you have still not completed the SEAS
Orientation Tutorials requirement in full, which is mandatory for all
Engineering graduate students. You are now past the deadline of Monday,
October 13, and will have a registration hold placed on your account next
week if you do not comply. Once the hold is placed, it will not be lifted
until the tutorials have been completed.

As a reminder, the Orientation Tutorials are available via CourseWorks and
can be accessed at the following link:
https://courseworks2.columbia.edu/courses/209703 . You must receive a
perfect score (100%) on every tutorial/quiz in order for this requirement
to be considered complete.

Please note that this requirement is in addition to the Pre-Orientation
Tutorials
<https://universitylife.columbia.edu/welcome-columbia-pre-orientation-tutorials >
and the University-wide Community Citizenship Initiative
<https://universitylife.columbia.edu/CCI >, both of which are mandated by
the Office of University Life.

Sincerely,
SEAS Academics
Engineering Student Affairs

emails.subject: October Carleton Commons Social this Friday! | EGSC Weekly Newsletter
emails.body:
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/  >
*Hey Grad SEAS!*
We hope you managed to dodge the downpour these past couple of days! With
the storm behind us and better (hopefully) weather rolling in, here's
what's upcoming with Grad SEAS!

This Friday, after our *General Body Meeting*, we're hosting our *October
Carleton Commons Social*! We're excited to see everyone there!

Don't forget to *follow us on Instagram @columbiaegsc

or not! We meet on Fridays from 4-5 pm in Mudd 524.
*Carleton Commons Social October Edition* is happening *this Friday*! Stop
by to grab some snacks, pizza, and drinks, and socialize with fellow SEAS
graduates!

This event is free and only for *current graduate engineering students*!
Registration is required and will take place at the door! *CUID with EP
sticker and government ID required!*
EGSC, in collaboration with SEAS graduate student organizations, is excited
to invite you to the *Fall Semester social at Ellington in the Park*!

Join us for a casual Thursday outing to enjoy the cozy fall evening!

This event includes food and drinks. Please obtain the ticket according to
your organization affiliation (i.e., if you are a Mechanical Engineering
student, you can only obtain a ticket from MEGA or EGSC). If you obtain a
ticket from the wrong organization, please notify us immediately, as your
admission to the event will not be counted!
RSVP Here!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.eventbrite.com/e/midsemester-social-tickets-1804269150599?aff=oddtdtcreator__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusR2IC0YI$ >

(CORE^2)* Eboard? Apply to be an executive member! This is a great
opportunity to utilize/gain leadership skills while simultaneously
enriching the way CORE^2 serves our members.

Join us in our mission to foster a community that provides academic,
social, and professional support for traditionally underrepresented
students in graduate engineering. We hope you are excited to fortify the
POC community in and around our university in these ways with us.

<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/1r6jLNyCo6pbxXR3jEqPT0a_FzcpqlB4d4iCH3_75ZbQ__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!U66AXcO3CK09cjy5CEinktcs0lyktt1ow3JxvVucDXBZcW_LeYTqdaFHhq0nJeCzhWoF8A1KI_e8L7oaewiVfhUko8lKWg$>

and
we'll be in touch about next steps! *

email: STEMStartersCU@gmail.com.
RSVP Here!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeJDkw_2wSSNtV-ajNrS97xSEPgzuBhy2XvVXUlVSJS1dpnGg/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusV3Y0nbo$ >
Looking for a hands-on, impactful internship next summer?YC just released
its Summer 2026 internship list, featuring roles at top AI startups where
you’ll work directly with founders, tackle meaningful projects, and gain
first-hand experience building the future of AI. Many past interns have
even gone on to launch their own startups and join YC as founders! Check
out the opportunities below!
YC Summer 2026 Internship List <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://znsrc.com/c/kfmvencctd__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nusqfh8VbY$ >
*We are thrilled to announce that applications for Nucleate's **Flagship
Activator Program* <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.org/activator/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusWIEUJHM$ >* are **NOW OPEN!*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusSLkOpuY$ >
Are you an academic trainee (Post-doc/PhD/MBA/MD/JD/MS) eager to build a
life science startup? Whether your passion lies in *therapeutics,
diagnostics, medtech, synthetic biology*, or fields like *food, energy,
agriculture, or consumer products*, this is your opportunity to transform
your research into a groundbreaking venture!
What We Offer:
●      *Completely Free:* Nucleate never takes equity or charges fees for
its programs
●      *Mentorship from Industry Leaders:* Receive guidance from
experienced mentors.
●      *Comprehensive Educational Programming:* Learn from the best in
workshops and seminars.
●      *Global Community: *Join a network of peers, alumni, and leaders
across 18+ cities worldwide.
●      *Investor Introductions:* Get connected with investors ready to
support your breakthrough.
●      *No Forced Incorporation:* Focus on your venture at your own pace.
If you have an idea (i.e., you’re an Inventor), you can apply for either
our *Bio* or *Eco* track:
●      *Bio Track*: For innovators in therapeutics, diagnostics, or tools.
●      *Eco Track*: Focused on food & agriculture, chemicals & energy, or
consumer products.
*Don’t have an idea yet?* No worries! You can still join a team as a
*scientific,
business, or clinical contributor* through our matching process.
*Got a team already?* Your collaborators can come on board as *team
affiliates*, so you all get the chance to learn and build together.
*Applications* <https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusSLkOpuY$ >* are reviewed on a rolling
basis through October 20. Early submission is encouraged for early team
matching*.
*Check out our **FAQs page*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://drive.google.com/file/d/1WV3LQJYHbdauBCrYzPAjDUarJPJGrK5a/view__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusC42KhTw$ >* if
you have any questions*
To learn more, join us at one of the following info sessions. Please
register to attend, food and drink will be served!

*What about IP?** Nucleate Activator is an education-focused, non-dilutive
program. We do not take any equity or IP, and all programming is designed
to be non-confidential. Inventors applying to the program must secure
approval from their PI and TTO before final acceptance.*
*About Nucleate*
Nucleate <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusZlGqvZM$ > is a student-run nonprofit organization
that facilitates the formation of new life science ventures. Our
equity-free Activator program <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/activator/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nus9wOApqk$ > supports
emerging bioentrepreneurs coming from academia who plan to tackle the
biggest challenges in human health (Bio Track) and sustainability (Eco
Track) by building life science companies. Since our inception seven years
ago, we have guided 600+ scientific teams through their earliest stages,
leading to the formation of more than 180 companies that have collectively
raised over $500M. Our alumni are commercializing breakthrough technologies
such as protein sequencing (Glyphic <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://glyphic.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusezQ3hpo$ >), AI drug design
(Manifold Bio <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.manifold.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nus4qL8Sks$ >, Concerto
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.concertobio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nus12AQYJk$ >), biomanufacturing (Foray Biosciences
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://foraybio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusdrwL_DM$ >) and many more.
*Apply*
Apply here <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusMNd91Tc$ > before *October 20th, 2025*.
*To Learn more about Nucleate visit our **Linktree*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://linktr.ee/nucleatehq__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusQVzqgrU$ >
[image: Instagram] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://instagram.com/columbiaegsc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nuse2CGxSs$ >
[image: LinkedIn] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/company/egsccolumbia__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nusu72KVjU$ >

<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/  >
*Hey Grad SEAS!*
We hope you managed to dodge the downpour these past couple of days! With
the storm behind us and better (hopefully) weather rolling in, here's
what's upcoming with Grad SEAS!

This Friday, after our *General Body Meeting*, we're hosting our *October
Carleton Commons Social*! We're excited to see everyone there!

<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.instagram.com/columbiaegsc/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusNrQV8Yo$ > *to stay updated on all our
events!

*Got a team already?* Your collaborators can come on board as *team
affiliates*, so you all get the chance to learn and build together.
*Applications* <https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusSLkOpuY$ >* are reviewed on a rolling
basis through October 20. Early submission is encouraged for early team
matching*.
*Check out our **FAQs page*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://drive.google.com/file/d/1WV3LQJYHbdauBCrYzPAjDUarJPJGrK5a/view__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusC42KhTw$ >* if
you have any questions*
To learn more, join us at one of the following info sessions. Please
register to attend, food and drink will be served!

*What about IP?** Nucleate Activator is an education-focused, non-dilutive
program. We do not take any equity or IP, and all programming is designed
to be non-confidential. Inventors applying to the program must secure
approval from their PI and TTO before final acceptance.*
*About Nucleate*
Nucleate <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusZlGqvZM$ > is a student-run nonprofit organization
that facilitates the formation of new life science ventures. Our
equity-free Activator program <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/activator/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nus9wOApqk$ > supports
emerging bioentrepreneurs coming from academia who plan to tackle the
biggest challenges in human health (Bio Track) and sustainability (Eco
Track) by building life science companies. Since our inception seven years
ago, we have guided 600+ scientific teams through their earliest stages,
leading to the formation of more than 180 companies that have collectively
raised over $500M. Our alumni are commercializing breakthrough technologies
such as protein sequencing (Glyphic <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://glyphic.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusezQ3hpo$ >), AI drug design
(Manifold Bio <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.manifold.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nus4qL8Sks$ >, Concerto
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.concertobio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nus12AQYJk$ >), biomanufacturing (Foray Biosciences
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://foraybio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusdrwL_DM$ >) and many more.
*Apply*
Apply here <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusMNd91Tc$ > before *October 20th, 2025*.
*To Learn more about Nucleate visit our **Linktree*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://linktr.ee/nucleatehq__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusQVzqgrU$ >

emails.subject: INVITATION: Symposium on AI and Finance, tomorrow, Friday, Oct. 3, 2025
emails.body:
Dear Engineers and Scientist,

On behalf of Dean, Shih-Fu Chang, we'd like to invite you to attend
tomorrow's Symposium on AI and Finance hosted by Columbia's Center for AI
and Responsible Financial Innovation, in collaboration with Capital One.
The event will take place at Faculty House from 9am to
4:30pm, tomorrow, Friday, Oct. 3, 2025.

Learn more and register at this link
<https://cairfi.engineering.columbia.edu/events/2025-cairfi-symposium-whats-next-ai-and-finance  >.
We look forward to seeing you!

   - 9:00 | Registration & Breakfast
   - 10:00 | Introductory Remarks
      - Shih-Fu Chang, Dean, Columbia Engineering
   - 10:10 | Keynote: *Indistinguishability and the Crystal Ball*
      - Cynthia Dwork, Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science, Harvard
      School of Engineering
   - 10:50 | Session: AI, Privacy, & Security in Finance
      - Rachel Cummings (Columbia Engineering), Junfeng Yang (Columbia
      Engineering), moderated by Stephen Rawls (Capital One)
   - 11:50 | Remarks
      - Prem Natarajan, Chief Scientist & Head of Enterprise AI, Capital One
   - 12:00 | Lunch & Poster Session
   - 1:30 | Session: AI and Causality
      - Elias Bareinboim (Columbia Engineering), Hannah Li (Columbia
      Business School), moderated by Agostino Capponi (Columbia Engineering)
   - 2:30 | Talk: The Future of Consumer Credit
      - Miao Wang (Columbia Engineering), moderated by Ali Hirsa (Columbia
      Engineering)
   - 3:00 | Panel: Future of AI & Fintech
      - Henry Yuen (Columbia Engineering), Atlas Wang (UT Austin), and
      Randall Balestriero (Brown University), moderated by Bayan Bruss (Capital
      One)
   - 4:15 | Closing Remarks

-- 

Andy Chae * He/Him/His *
Senior Director of Graduate Student Life
Engineering Student Affairs
Columbia Engineering

ac4897@columbia.edu  |  212-854-8930  |  Columbia Engineering

emails.subject: Explore Careers in Engineering & Scientific Consulting with Exponent (Oct 13)
emails.body:
Dear PhD Students,

The Engineering Graduate Student Council (EGSC) is pleased to invite you to
a special seminar with Exponent, a premier engineering and scientific
consulting firm known for solving some of the world’s most complex
technical challenges.

This event is designed to help PhD students explore how their research
skills translate into impactful consulting careers. You’ll hear from
Exponent experts about the innovative projects they lead and learn how
advanced technical backgrounds contribute to solving real-world problems
across industries. It’s also a great opportunity to connect directly with
the team and learn more about career pathways and job opportunities at
Exponent.

🔹 *Date:* Monday, October 13, 2025
🔹 *Time:* 12:00 - 1:00 PM
🔹 *Location:* Hamilton 603
🔹 Lunch will be provided

*Featured Speakers:*

   -

   *Dr. Courtney Petersen* - *Senior Associate, Biomedical Engineering &
   Sciences*
   Focused on medical device evaluation and MRI compatibility through
   computational and experimental methods.
   -

   *Dr. Khaled Hashad* - *Senior Engineer, Thermal Sciences*
   Specializes in CFD, heat transfer, and machine learning applications for
   failure analysis and thermal management.
   -

   *Ms. Sarah Dahlberg* - *Managing Scientist, Health Sciences*
   Expert in regulatory affairs, pharmacovigilance, and human health risk
   assessment for pharmaceuticals and biotechnology.

Whether you’re curious about consulting or actively exploring your next
step after graduate school, this seminar offers valuable insight into how
Exponent’s collaborative environment empowers PhD students to thrive in the
industry.

👉 *RSVP here:* Register Now
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://exponentrecruit.azurewebsites.net/Register/302__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!SV5xJ-dRS6Hnt9qaseBWp5ys9ejsyo0RG-WmV4-AyB8FZo9pmXJg7T_lFxmtINlQVG8P-fFr7NIoIh9YaGjtG5Ok3QnyLvP5lwqhDFiFSvK-mK1nhcgiO7s$ >
[image: Columbia - Exponent Seminar - October 13. 2025 - Flyer
(Updated).jpg]

*Kindly see the attached flyer for additional details.*

We hope to see you there!

*With Regards, Dhruv Bhatt, M.S.*
* <http://egsc.seas.columbia.edu/  >*
*Career & Alumni Chair, *
*Engineering Graduate Student Co <http://egsc.seas.columbia.edu/  >**uncil
(EGSC <http://egsc.seas.columbia.edu/  >)*
*Columbia Engineering, Mechanical Engineering*
*He/ Him/ His*

emails.subject: October 30 Faculty Panel - Invitation
emails.body:
Dear Doctoral Students,

We are pleased to share the upcoming session, *Faculty Lives, Faculty
Careers: Stories from the Academic Job Market*, part of Columbia
University’s *Academic Application & Early Career Success Series*,
organized by the Office for Postdoctoral Affairs and the Zuckerman
Institute.
*Thursday, October 30, 2025*
*3:30 – 5:00 PM*
*Jerome L. Greene Science Center, 9th Floor*
* RSVP HERE
<https://events.columbia.edu/cal/event/eventView.do?b=de&calPath=/public/cals/MainCal&guid=CAL-00bbdb71-9970783f-0199-72646c8a-00004d15events@columbia.edu&recurrenceId=  >*
(Registration closes October 29 at 12:00 PM)

This 90-minute panel will bring together faculty at different career stages
to share their firsthand experiences navigating the academic job market,
from preparing applications to interviewing and transitioning into new
roles. Attendees will gain practical insights and strategies for success at
each stage of the process.

Please note: This session will not be recorded.

Best,

*Richard Hagen, Ph.D.* *(He/Him)*
Assistant Director, Graduate Student Affairs
Engineering Student Affairs
Columbia Engineering

emails.subject: LinkedIn Photoshoot, Cocoa House, and PDS | EGSC Weekly Newsletter
emails.body:
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/  >
*Hey Grad SEAS!*

It was great seeing everyone at the *Fall 2025* *Boat Cruise* last Friday!
We all had such a great time 🫰🩵🦁💙🫰

*📸 *Tickets for today's *LinkedIn Photoshoot* are *SOLD OUT 📸*
🍪☕️ We are hosting *EGSC Cocoa House* on *Thursday *☕️🍪
📖 Apply for the *Professional Development Scholarship* by *October 11th* 📖

Don't forget to *follow us on Instagram @columbiaegsc
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.instagram.com/columbiaegsc/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHfSPMDko$ > *to stay updated on all our
events!
*Pictures from the Graduate Campus Life Fair are Here!*
View the Album Here!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/11FCvPNSHrN6gHUwS58HSvVtNKpb-b92u?usp=sharing__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHCb-yPRc$ >
Tickets for the *LinkedIn Photoshoot* are *SOLD OUT*! Please stay tuned for
more opportunities to get your professional headshot taken throughout the
semester!
*Caffeinate and conversate* with other students from graduate SEAS! Enjoy
coffee, cookies, and some great company *this Thursday* at *EGSC Cocoa
House*!
Join us at our *weekly GBMs*, whether you are a Department Representative
or not! We meet on Fridays from 4-5 pm in Mudd 524.

Are you a current Columbia University graduate student founder eager to
pitch your business idea? Or are you looking to connect with other founders
and like-minded peers? If so, join us for an inspiring evening of pitches,
networking, and conversation!

Co-sponsored by the *Lang Center at Columbia Business School *and* the
Engineering Graduate Student Council*, this event offers a unique
opportunity to share ideas, meet fellow entrepreneurs, and build meaningful
connections across Columbia’s schools.

How It Works:

*When you register, you’ll answer a few quick questions—including whether
you’d like to pitch. *If selected, you’ll have the chance to showcase your
idea and meet potential collaborators. After the pitches, we’ll transition
into a relaxed mixer where you can continue networking.

This is a *casual and welcoming* environment—just bring yourself and your
enthusiasm! Whether you're actively working on a venture or simply curious,
we’d love to have you join us.

We look forward to seeing you there!

*THE ENTREPRENEURSHIP MIXER & PITCH NIGHT IS SOLD OUT!*
Join the Waitlist Here! <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/jsoog0en__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHR4F_m4k$ >
In keeping with the highest ideals of the EGSC, the *Professional
Development Scholarship Program* is awarded to outstanding graduate
students in the Columbia University Fu Foundation School of Engineering and
Applied Science (SEAS) as a means of defraying costs associated with their
professional endeavors.  Those chosen as EGSC Professional Development
Scholars are expected to make a broader impact on the larger Columbia
Engineering community by sharing their knowledge and experience.

Past recipients have used the Scholarship to fund *memberships* *to
professional societies, certification exam fees, and the costs of attending
academic or professional conferences*.

*AWARD AMOUNT:*
Applicants may request *up to $1,000* per application. The final award
amount and the number of awards per semester will be decided by the
evaluating committee. Any unused funds are non-transferable and may not be
used for expenses unrelated to those explicitly stated in the funded
application.

Please note that original receipts and proof of acceptance/attendance (if
applicable) are required in order to be eligible for reimbursement.
Furthermore, for travel expenses, stays exceeding 1 day before and after
the event must be justified and will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

*FALL APPLICATION DEADLINE:*
*Saturday, October 11th, 2025 at 5:00PM EST.*
Apply Here!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScukPIxcTNSLHAOkoMMPw8z1UPCiQgjb6sLuwFhgsV7JEPqDQ/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHn65Mc2w$ >
Columbia Product Managers Club (PMC) and Columbia Entrepreneurship,
Leadership & Consulting (ELC) Club invite you to an evening of networking
and discovery.

Whether you’re passionate about *product management, consulting, or
entrepreneurship*, this mixer is the perfect place to connect with peers,
exchange ideas, and explore future opportunities.

✨ Expect meaningful conversations, new connections across disciplines, and
a chance to learn from fellow students shaping careers at the intersection
of tech, business, and innovation.

🥂 *Food & Drinks Provided* – come for the networking, stay for the good
vibes!

📍 *Location: *The Craftsman
📅 *Date:* Wednesday, October 1st, 2025
🕡 *Time:* 6:30 – 7:30 PM

🤝 *Hosted by:* Columbia Product Managers Club (PMC) x Columbia
Entrepreneurship, Leadership & Consulting (ELC) Club.

🔗 RSVP now — *scan the QR code on the poster!*

📨 Stay tuned and follow us for updates:
📧 *pmccolumbia@gmail.com <pmccolumbia@gmail.com>*
📸 Instagram: @columbiapmc
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://instagram.com/columbiapmc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Wa1FqtnIYlf9dXmH8b4FCUl4MnoVUxEHEIhbSo2YJ3_M4dwQAytWcrQfPCFORNNSg-tSEqPRrB8KrO_IebEVvuJ01UlIZ7df0im08YQ$>
💼 LinkedIn: Columbia Product Managers Club (PMC)
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/company/columbia-pmc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Wa1FqtnIYlf9dXmH8b4FCUl4MnoVUxEHEIhbSo2YJ3_M4dwQAytWcrQfPCFORNNSg-tSEqPRrB8KrO_IebEVvuJ01UlIZ7dfBe5eENk$>
Interested in joining the *Coalition on Race and Ethnicity in Engineering
(CORE^2)* Eboard? Apply to be an executive member! This is a great
opportunity to utilize/gain leadership skills while simultaneously
enriching the way CORE^2 serves our members.

Join us in our mission to foster a community that provides academic,
social, and professional support for traditionally underrepresented
students in graduate engineering. We hope you are excited to fortify the
POC community in and around our university in these ways with us.
Apply Today!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSewY0za5v5I_UQXX_n_Ypt_D537mwAup-z4ZWgLm2KBdjP6SA/viewform?usp=dialog__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!TruXzRtu85xKuJPIemjZDvW0sxaZqv-xt6ZMR2O_VeI4vSVRNUjkjGOcdDHciOnzbbysr4hOKJ2Bi6rvsJ55vXcA$>
STEM Starters accepts both Columbia post-doc and graduate student
volunteers for our free science events for kids! All volunteers must go
through Protection of Minors training (~1hr) for the safety of our
attendees.

*Please record your email here
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSe6d2t9FB4bdFJtgd3aD7DeLob6h8ZbKZmVrGWJF4HjS0uQMA/viewform?pli=1__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHPCVHjUU$ >
and
we'll be in touch about next steps! *

For more information, visit our website at:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.womeninscienceatcolumbia.org/stemstarters__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzH9zuYmtM$  or contact us by
email: STEMStartersCU@gmail.com.

*** No-cost flu vaccines at these events are available to ALL ELIGIBLE
STUDENTS* <https://www.health.columbia.edu/flu#text-18266  > regardless of
credit load or health insurance plan.No appointment needed: just bring your
CUID.***

*Monday, Sep. 29 - Wednesday, Oct. 1: Low Library Rotunda*
*Enter via the Northeast entrance only.
<https://www.health.columbia.edu/sites/default/files/content/pics/Flu-Fair/Map%20to%20NE%20Entrance%20from%20Sundial.png  >*
Monday, Sep. 29: 10:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m.
Tuesday, Sep. 30: 7:30 a.m. - 4:30 p.m.
Wednesday, Oct. 1: 9:00 a.m - 6:30 p.m.

*Wednesday, October 8: 3rd Fl. Board Rooms, Geffen Hall (Manhattanville)*
9:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m.

Please note that you are not able to join the line prior to opening or
after closing.

For those over 65 years of age
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.cdc.gov/flu/highrisk/65over.htm__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X7f859X_d379DEJVUzq_6MK7S-OiquHc7Pmxyfto6wyujDDIv8qCILkURv8l3bSzvpIpCPWNTPJ17JFIMV1EVglHHG9ALunTdXs$>,
Columbia Health has a limited supply of the high-dose vaccine. Please speak
to the nurse administering your vaccine if you are eligible for the
high-dose flu vaccine.

***Early bird exclusive****
Each day, the first 150 community members who get their flu shot at the
event will receive an exclusive “Fight Flu” hot/cold pack!
*CTV, Columbia’s technology transfer office, is hiring graduate students
for the Fellows Program
<https://techventures.columbia.edu/about-ctv/ctv-fellows-program  >*. This is
a unique internship opportunity that introduces scientists to technology
commercialization. Fellows work closely with CTV on a wide range of
projects, including assessment of the commercial viability of new
inventions, identifying potential licensees or investors, developing
marketing materials, and analysis of patent literature. Fellows are paid
$28.46 per hour.

*Applications for the Fellows Program opened on 9/17/2025*. The first round
of applications is due *10/2/2025 by 5 pm EDT*. Info sessions will be held
on Sept. 15 at 3 pm and Sept. 18 at 10 am. Both will be Zoom meetings held
on this link: https://columbiauniversity.zoom.us/my/ctvfellows  .

Looking for a hands-on, impactful internship next summer?YC just released
its Summer 2026 internship list, featuring roles at top AI startups where
you’ll work directly with founders, tackle meaningful projects, and gain
first-hand experience building the future of AI. Many past interns have
even gone on to launch their own startups and join YC as founders! Check
out the opportunities below!
YC Summer 2026 Internship List <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://znsrc.com/c/kfmvencctd__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHzsXekPs$ >
*We are thrilled to announce that applications for Nucleate's **Flagship
Activator Program* <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.org/activator/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHCA2WLv8$ >* are **NOW OPEN!*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHecHcL24$ >
Are you an academic trainee (Post-doc/PhD/MBA/MD/JD/MS) eager to build a
life science startup? Whether your passion lies in *therapeutics,
diagnostics, medtech, synthetic biology*, or fields like *food, energy,
agriculture, or consumer products*, this is your opportunity to transform
your research into a groundbreaking venture!
What We Offer:
●      *Completely Free:* Nucleate never takes equity or charges fees for
its programs
●      *Mentorship from Industry Leaders:* Receive guidance from
experienced mentors.
●      *Comprehensive Educational Programming:* Learn from the best in
workshops and seminars.
●      *Global Community: *Join a network of peers, alumni, and leaders
across 18+ cities worldwide.
●      *Investor Introductions:* Get connected with investors ready to
support your breakthrough.
●      *No Forced Incorporation:* Focus on your venture at your own pace.
If you have an idea (i.e., you’re an Inventor), you can apply for either
our *Bio* or *Eco* track:
●      *Bio Track*: For innovators in therapeutics, diagnostics, or tools.
●      *Eco Track*: Focused on food & agriculture, chemicals & energy, or
consumer products.
*Don’t have an idea yet?* No worries! You can still join a team as a
*scientific,
business, or clinical contributor* through our matching process.
*Got a team already?* Your collaborators can come on board as *team
affiliates*, so you all get the chance to learn and build together.
*Applications* <https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHecHcL24$ >* are reviewed on a rolling
basis through October 20. Early submission is encouraged for early team
matching*.
*Check out our **FAQs page*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://drive.google.com/file/d/1WV3LQJYHbdauBCrYzPAjDUarJPJGrK5a/view__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHXUyRaxk$ >* if
you have any questions*
To learn more, join us at one of the following info sessions. Please
register to attend, food and drink will be served!

   1. Columbia Morningside

*What about IP?** Nucleate Activator is an education-focused, non-dilutive
program. We do not take any equity or IP, and all programming is designed
to be non-confidential. Inventors applying to the program must secure
approval from their PI and TTO before final acceptance.*
*About Nucleate*
Nucleate <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHqgN4iV8$ > is a student-run nonprofit organization
that facilitates the formation of new life science ventures. Our
equity-free Activator program <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/activator/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHNUQunGI$ > supports
emerging bioentrepreneurs coming from academia who plan to tackle the
biggest challenges in human health (Bio Track) and sustainability (Eco
Track) by building life science companies. Since our inception seven years
ago, we have guided 600+ scientific teams through their earliest stages,
leading to the formation of more than 180 companies that have collectively
raised over $500M. Our alumni are commercializing breakthrough technologies
such as protein sequencing (Glyphic <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://glyphic.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHAKoyiLE$ >), AI drug design
(Manifold Bio <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.manifold.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHsNDABnQ$ >, Concerto
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.concertobio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHWWFzlvU$ >), biomanufacturing (Foray Biosciences
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://foraybio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHR_jElTk$ >) and many more.
*Apply*
Apply here <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzH9n9veE0$ > before *October 20th, 2025*.

more opportunities to get your professional headshot taken throughout the
semester!
*Caffeinate and conversate* with other students from graduate SEAS! Enjoy
coffee, cookies, and some great company *this Thursday* at *EGSC Cocoa
House*!
Join us at our *weekly GBMs*, whether you are a Department Representative
or not! We meet on Fridays from 4-5 pm in Mudd 524.

Are you a current Columbia University graduate student founder eager to
pitch your business idea? Or are you looking to connect with other founders
and like-minded peers? If so, join us for an inspiring evening of pitches,
networking, and conversation!

Co-sponsored by the *Lang Center at Columbia Business School *and* the
Engineering Graduate Student Council*, this event offers a unique
opportunity to share ideas, meet fellow entrepreneurs, and build meaningful
connections across Columbia’s schools.

How It Works:

*When you register, you’ll answer a few quick questions—including whether
you’d like to pitch. *If selected, you’ll have the chance to showcase your
idea and meet potential collaborators. After the pitches, we’ll transition
into a relaxed mixer where you can continue networking.

This is a *casual and welcoming* environment—just bring yourself and your
enthusiasm! Whether you're actively working on a venture or simply curious,
we’d love to have you join us.

We look forward to seeing you there!

*THE ENTREPRENEURSHIP MIXER & PITCH NIGHT IS SOLD OUT!*
Join the Waitlist Here! <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/jsoog0en__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHR4F_m4k$ >
In keeping with the highest ideals of the EGSC, the *Professional
Development Scholarship Program* is awarded to outstanding graduate
students in the Columbia University Fu Foundation School of Engineering and
Applied Science (SEAS) as a means of defraying costs associated with their
professional endeavors.  Those chosen as EGSC Professional Development
Scholars are expected to make a broader impact on the larger Columbia
Engineering community by sharing their knowledge and experience.

Past recipients have used the Scholarship to fund *memberships* *to
professional societies, certification exam fees, and the costs of attending
academic or professional conferences*.

*AWARD AMOUNT:*
Applicants may request *up to $1,000* per application. The final award
amount and the number of awards per semester will be decided by the
evaluating committee. Any unused funds are non-transferable and may not be
used for expenses unrelated to those explicitly stated in the funded
application.

Please note that original receipts and proof of acceptance/attendance (if
applicable) are required in order to be eligible for reimbursement.
Furthermore, for travel expenses, stays exceeding 1 day before and after
the event must be justified and will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

*FALL APPLICATION DEADLINE:*
*Saturday, October 11th, 2025 at 5:00PM EST.*
Apply Here!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScukPIxcTNSLHAOkoMMPw8z1UPCiQgjb6sLuwFhgsV7JEPqDQ/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHn65Mc2w$ >

*CTV, Columbia’s technology transfer office, is hiring graduate students
for the Fellows Program
<https://techventures.columbia.edu/about-ctv/ctv-fellows-program  >*. This is
a unique internship opportunity that introduces scientists to technology
commercialization. Fellows work closely with CTV on a wide range of
projects, including assessment of the commercial viability of new
inventions, identifying potential licensees or investors, developing
marketing materials, and analysis of patent literature. Fellows are paid
$28.46 per hour.

*Applications for the Fellows Program opened on 9/17/2025*. The first round
of applications is due *10/2/2025 by 5 pm EDT*. Info sessions will be held
on Sept. 15 at 3 pm and Sept. 18 at 10 am. Both will be Zoom meetings held
on this link: https://columbiauniversity.zoom.us/my/ctvfellows  .

Looking for a hands-on, impactful internship next summer?YC just released
its Summer 2026 internship list, featuring roles at top AI startups where
you’ll work directly with founders, tackle meaningful projects, and gain
first-hand experience building the future of AI. Many past interns have
even gone on to launch their own startups and join YC as founders! Check
out the opportunities below!
YC Summer 2026 Internship List <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://znsrc.com/c/kfmvencctd__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHzsXekPs$ >
*We are thrilled to announce that applications for Nucleate's **Flagship
Activator Program* <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.org/activator/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHCA2WLv8$ >* are **NOW OPEN!*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHecHcL24$ >
Are you an academic trainee (Post-doc/PhD/MBA/MD/JD/MS) eager to build a
life science startup? Whether your passion lies in *therapeutics,
diagnostics, medtech, synthetic biology*, or fields like *food, energy,
agriculture, or consumer products*, this is your opportunity to transform
your research into a groundbreaking venture!
What We Offer:
●      *Completely Free:* Nucleate never takes equity or charges fees for
its programs
●      *Mentorship from Industry Leaders:* Receive guidance from
experienced mentors.
●      *Comprehensive Educational Programming:* Learn from the best in
workshops and seminars.
●      *Global Community: *Join a network of peers, alumni, and leaders
across 18+ cities worldwide.
●      *Investor Introductions:* Get connected with investors ready to
support your breakthrough.
●      *No Forced Incorporation:* Focus on your venture at your own pace.
If you have an idea (i.e., you’re an Inventor), you can apply for either
our *Bio* or *Eco* track:
●      *Bio Track*: For innovators in therapeutics, diagnostics, or tools.
●      *Eco Track*: Focused on food & agriculture, chemicals & energy, or
consumer products.
*Don’t have an idea yet?* No worries! You can still join a team as a
*scientific,
business, or clinical contributor* through our matching process.
*Got a team already?* Your collaborators can come on board as *team
affiliates*, so you all get the chance to learn and build together.
*Applications* <https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHecHcL24$ >* are reviewed on a rolling
basis through October 20. Early submission is encouraged for early team
matching*.
*Check out our **FAQs page*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://drive.google.com/file/d/1WV3LQJYHbdauBCrYzPAjDUarJPJGrK5a/view__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHXUyRaxk$ >* if
you have any questions*
To learn more, join us at one of the following info sessions. Please
register to attend, food and drink will be served!

   1. Columbia Morningside
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/64sdaqgf?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHK6vT-YY$ >Info Session:
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/64sdaqgf?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHK6vT-YY$ > Tuesday, September
   30, 5:30 PM at Davis Auditorium
   2. Columbia Medical Center
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/xwnoz3hs?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHDcSlz8U$ > Info Session:
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/xwnoz3hs?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHDcSlz8U$ > Thursday, October
   2, 5:30 PM at Alexandria LaunchLabs

Can’t make the in-person session? *Sign up for a virtual info session or
Live Q&A session with Activator Alumni and Mentors*:

   1. Virtual Info Session:
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/n5t938v2?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHcCE5rME$ >  Thursday,
   September 18, at 12 PM
   2. Nucleate Activator - Insider Session:
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/pcjqooak?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzH6W0dgWY$ > Tuesday, October
   7, at 6 PM

Alternatively, join us at one of our *events*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://lu.ma/nucleate-ny-community-events__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzH2oNC3o4$ > in NYC!

   1. *Connect and Collab: Fall Mixer with NYAS*
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/c4fiyh8y?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHkOypDBU$ > Thursday,
   September 25th, 6 PM at NYAS

*What about IP?** Nucleate Activator is an education-focused, non-dilutive
program. We do not take any equity or IP, and all programming is designed
to be non-confidential. Inventors applying to the program must secure
approval from their PI and TTO before final acceptance.*
*About Nucleate*
Nucleate <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHqgN4iV8$ > is a student-run nonprofit organization
that facilitates the formation of new life science ventures. Our
equity-free Activator program <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/activator/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHNUQunGI$ > supports
emerging bioentrepreneurs coming from academia who plan to tackle the
biggest challenges in human health (Bio Track) and sustainability (Eco
Track) by building life science companies. Since our inception seven years
ago, we have guided 600+ scientific teams through their earliest stages,
leading to the formation of more than 180 companies that have collectively
raised over $500M. Our alumni are commercializing breakthrough technologies
such as protein sequencing (Glyphic <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://glyphic.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHAKoyiLE$ >), AI drug design
(Manifold Bio <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.manifold.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHsNDABnQ$ >, Concerto
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.concertobio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHWWFzlvU$ >), biomanufacturing (Foray Biosciences
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://foraybio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHR_jElTk$ >) and many more.
*Apply*
Apply here <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzH9n9veE0$ > before *October 20th, 2025*.
*To Learn more about Nucleate visit our **Linktree*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://linktr.ee/nucleatehq__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHjhitSBI$ >
[image: Instagram] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://instagram.com/columbiaegsc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHx5uMQX8$ >
[image: LinkedIn] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/company/egsccolumbia__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHn48dQv0$ >

emails.subject: Get your free flu shot next week at Low Library!
emails.body:
Dear Karthik,

  

Following up on the message sent by Dr. Bernitz, I’d like to encourage you to get your no-cost flu shot next week. The flu virus is already circulating in New York City, and the best way to protect ourselves and our family, friends, and colleagues is to get the flu shot.

  

Starting next Monday Sep. 29 through Wednesday Oct. 1, you can go to Low Library to get a no-cost flu shot. Walk-in only, just bring your CUID. If you can’t make it next week, there is an additional opportunity on the Manhattanville campus on Oct. 8.

  

Monday, Sep. 29 - Wednesday, Oct. 1: Low Library Rotunda

Enter via the Northeast entrance only.

Monday, Sep. 29: 10:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m.

Tuesday, Sep. 30: 7:30 a.m. - 4:30 p.m.

Wednesday, Oct. 1: 9:00 a.m - 6:30 p.m.

  

Wednesday, Oct. 8: 3rd Fl. Board Rooms, Geffen Hall

9:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m.

   

Please note that you are not able to join the line prior to opening or after closing.

  

If you have specific concerns about receiving a flu vaccine, please connect with us at Medical Services: call 212-854-7426, ext. 4 and request to speak with a care team member who will provide guidance.


Thank you for taking this opportunity to keep yourself and our community healthy.

  

Be well,

Urmi Desai
Associate Vice President, Medical Services

emails.subject: Updated invitation: 🌮 DAPLab Seminar and Lunch - Guest Speaker Kyunghyun Cho @ Tue Sep 30, 2025 12pm - 1:30pm (EDT) (Karthik Sreedhar)
emails.body:
This event has been updated
Changed: title, description

🌮 DAPLab Seminar and Lunch - Guest Speaker Kyunghyun Cho
Tuesday Sep 30, 2025 ⋅ 12pm – 1:30pm
Eastern Time - New York

Location
453 Mudd/CSB	
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.google.com/maps/search/453*Mudd*2FCSB?hl=en__;KyU!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RwosTY2AM6qNj9ts4JVbhhArQJdfTRv4EujH36PkKLOdOmQZDsu42lKnrXI40r2-LuvDtxNZSfp2uOnLzZaxGkvZ$ 

Attachments
2025 Fall DAPLab  Seminar Agenda  
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1wUzL8H6R6DUbXh2hhsI64l7lvNFddWCaFViEZ_Ryn10/edit?slide=id.g37d27ac9933_0_1__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RwosTY2AM6qNj9ts4JVbhhArQJdfTRv4EujH36PkKLOdOmQZDsu42lKnrXI40r2-LuvDtxNZSfp2uOnLzTSSV8QQ$ 

2025 Fall DAPLab  Seminar AgendaAbstractDespite its amazing success,  
leaderboard chasing has become something researchers dread and mock. When  
implemented properly and executed faithfully, leaderboard chasing can lead  
to both faster and easily reproducible progress in science, as evident from  
the amazing progress we have seen with machine learning, or more broadly  
artificial intelligence, in recent decades. It does not however mean that  
it is easy to implement and execute leaderboard chasing properly. In this  
talk, I will go over four case studies demonstrating the issues that  
ultimately prevent leaderboard chasing from a valid scientific approach.  
The first case study is on the lack of proper hyperparameter tuning in  
continual learning, the second on the lack of consensus on evaluation  
metrics in machine unlearning, the third on the challenges of properly  
evaluating the evaluation metrics in free-form text generation, and the  
final one on wishful thinking. By going over these cases, I hope we can  
collectively acknowledge some of our own fallacies, think of underlying  
causes behind these fallacies and come up with better ways to approach  
artificial intelligence research.Speaker BioKyunghyun Cho is a professor of  
computer science and data science at New York University and an executive  
director of frontier research at the Prescient Design team within Genentech  
Research & Early Development (gRED). He became the Glen de Vries  
Professor of Health Statistics in 2025. He is also a CIFAR Fellow of  
Learning in Machines & Brains and an Associate Member of the National  
Academy of Engineering of Korea. He served as a (co-)Program Chair of ICLR  
2020, NeurIPS 2022 and ICML 2022. He was one of the three founding  
Editors-in-Chief of the Transactions on Machine Learning Research (TMLR)  
until 2024. He was a research scientist at Facebook AI Research from June  
2017 to May 2020 and a postdoctoral fellow at University of Montreal until  
Summer 2015 under the supervision of Prof. Yoshua Bengio, after receiving  
MSc and PhD degrees from Aalto University April 2011 and April 2014,  
respectively, under the supervision of Prof. Juha Karhunen, Dr. Tapani  
Raiko and Dr. Alexander Ilin. He received the Samsung Ho-Am Prize in  
Engineering in 2021. He tries his best to find a balance among machine  
learning, natural language processing, and life, but almost always fails to  
do so.

Guests
Eugene Wu - creator
Luis Gravano
Haonan Wang
Karthik Sreedhar
Daniel Joseph Hsu
Edward Guo
Yuhang Wu
Daplab-members

Xiao Yu

emails.snippet: 🌮 DAPLab Seminar and Lunch - Guest Speaker Kyunghyun Cho 2025 Fall DAPLab Seminar AgendaAbstractDespite its amazing success, leaderboard chasing has become something researchers dread and mock. When
emails.originalBody:
This event has been updated
Changed: title, description

🌮 DAPLab Seminar and Lunch - Guest Speaker Kyunghyun Cho
Tuesday Sep 30, 2025 ⋅ 12pm – 1:30pm
Eastern Time - New York

Location
453 Mudd/CSB	
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.google.com/maps/search/453*Mudd*2FCSB?hl=en__;KyU!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RwosTY2AM6qNj9ts4JVbhhArQJdfTRv4EujH36PkKLOdOmQZDsu42lKnrXI40r2-LuvDtxNZSfp2uOnLzZaxGkvZ$ 

Attachments
2025 Fall DAPLab  Seminar Agenda  
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1wUzL8H6R6DUbXh2hhsI64l7lvNFddWCaFViEZ_Ryn10/edit?slide=id.g37d27ac9933_0_1__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RwosTY2AM6qNj9ts4JVbhhArQJdfTRv4EujH36PkKLOdOmQZDsu42lKnrXI40r2-LuvDtxNZSfp2uOnLzTSSV8QQ$ 

2025 Fall DAPLab  Seminar AgendaAbstractDespite its amazing success,  
leaderboard chasing has become something researchers dread and mock. When  
implemented properly and executed faithfully, leaderboard chasing can lead  
to both faster and easily reproducible progress in science, as evident from  
the amazing progress we have seen with machine learning, or more broadly  
artificial intelligence, in recent decades. It does not however mean that  
it is easy to implement and execute leaderboard chasing properly. In this  
talk, I will go over four case studies demonstrating the issues that  
ultimately prevent leaderboard chasing from a valid scientific approach.  
The first case study is on the lack of proper hyperparameter tuning in  
continual learning, the second on the lack of consensus on evaluation  
metrics in machine unlearning, the third on the challenges of properly  
evaluating the evaluation metrics in free-form text generation, and the  
final one on wishful thinking. By going over these cases, I hope we can  
collectively acknowledge some of our own fallacies, think of underlying  
causes behind these fallacies and come up with better ways to approach  
artificial intelligence research.Speaker BioKyunghyun Cho is a professor of  
computer science and data science at New York University and an executive  
director of frontier research at the Prescient Design team within Genentech  
Research & Early Development (gRED). He became the Glen de Vries  
Professor of Health Statistics in 2025. He is also a CIFAR Fellow of  
Learning in Machines & Brains and an Associate Member of the National  
Academy of Engineering of Korea. He served as a (co-)Program Chair of ICLR  
2020, NeurIPS 2022 and ICML 2022. He was one of the three founding  
Editors-in-Chief of the Transactions on Machine Learning Research (TMLR)  
until 2024. He was a research scientist at Facebook AI Research from June  
2017 to May 2020 and a postdoctoral fellow at University of Montreal until  
Summer 2015 under the supervision of Prof. Yoshua Bengio, after receiving  
MSc and PhD degrees from Aalto University April 2011 and April 2014,  
respectively, under the supervision of Prof. Juha Karhunen, Dr. Tapani  
Raiko and Dr. Alexander Ilin. He received the Samsung Ho-Am Prize in  
Engineering in 2021. He tries his best to find a balance among machine  
learning, natural language processing, and life, but almost always fails to  
do so.

emails.subject: EGSC Boat Cruise – Important Security & Documentation Reminder
emails.body:
Dear Students,

We hope you are excited for tonight’s EGSC Boat Cruise! To help ensure
everything runs smoothly, please keep the following security reminders in
mind:

   -

   *Do not bring items that could be viewed as weapons* (e.g., mace,
   scissors, or other sharp objects).
   -

   *Avoid overly large bags or backpacks* to help ensure a quick and smooth
   security check.
   -

   *Bring only physical, government-issued identification* (passport, visa,
   or driver’s license). *Digital copies of age verification documents will
   not be accepted.*

We can’t wait to see you tonight!

Sincerely,

Seas-Life

emails.body:
This email is to notify you that you have been signed up for the following study by Lucie Clifford:

Study Name: Velmara Study
Date: Monday, September 29, 2025 12:00 PM - 12:07 PM
Location: Behavioral Research Lab (Multipurpose, Uris B204)
Researchers: Derek Brown <ndb2137@gsb.columbia.edu>, Caden Burkman <crb2227@columbia.edu>, Sophie Cheng <sqc2103@barnard.edu>, Sania Cunningham <sc5399@barnard.edu>, Zahra Kamal <zhk2108@barnard.edu>, Aria Simon <mbs2252@barnard.edu>

You may cancel your sign-up until Sunday, September 28, 2025 12:00 PM.

emails.subject: Re: Fall 2025 Cycle: Professional Development Scholarship
emails.body:
Awesome! I just sent it Sitong Vivian and Tao :)

On Thu, Sep 25, 2025 at 19:33 Karthik Sreedhar <ks4190@columbia.edu> wrote:

> I can't apply to this because I got it in the spring for IUI, but in case
> anyone else is traveling to a conference it might be helpful!
>
> ---------- Forwarded message ---------
> From: Priyanka Dilip <treasurer@egsc.seas.columbia.edu>
> Date: Thu, Sep 25, 2025 at 12:20 PM
> Subject: Fall 2025 Cycle: Professional Development Scholarship
> To: <SEASGRAD-MORN@lists.columbia.edu>
>
>
> 'Afternoon y'all!
>
> I write to you to announce the opening of the Fall 2025 cycle of the Professional
> Development Scholarship Program
> <https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/content/professional-development-scholarship-spring-2025 > -
> awarded to outstanding graduate students in the Columbia University Fu
> Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science (SEAS) to defray costs
> associated with their professional endeavors. Those chosen as
> EGSC Professional Development Scholars are expected to make a broader
> impact on the larger Columbia Engineering community by sharing their
> knowledge and experience.
>
> Past recipients have used the Scholarship to fund the charges of attending
> academic or professional conferences, memberships
> to professional societies, and certification/examination fees.
>
> Due to popular demand, students will be eligible to apply for the
> scholarship to cover programs between September 1st, 2025, and December
> 31st, 2025. Payment in advance, instead of reimbursement after the fact,
> will be considered on an exceptional case-by-case basis. Apply Today
> <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=http-3A__bit.ly_pds-2Degsc-2Dspring-2D2025&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=_sOOmOtoxrhy7bBV2eo7i6NA6-LDb1PqjHbXXd9DLyQ&m=aI2UCX4P87f7soRvNtfI8FAV_vEaRJzpVf5xrschtbfv6amQg1JG5QFyED29HpzK&s=FUw6DvRLp5Ue_Me_S2Q9xKTPqEkLwtwpAlgqEJ6QTmk&e= >
>  - bit.ly/pds-egsc-fall-2025
> <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://forms.gle/TzGjvJ4GkUMPpRJR6__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Wexnr8SIsX-6PbAChGBq2RjUgPaKqZt8ZlebPCVAy5DabEXmlXVZ329DLv4nqo7hsvJc_1riV62Grk62gQC2Dro3zQZ5G_MHS2EPUElP_6I$>
>
> Deadline: Saturday, October 11th, 2025 at 5PM ET.
>
> For further inquiry, read the FAQ
> <https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/content/professional-development-scholarship-fall-2023 > OR
> contact the EGSC Treasurer, Priyanka Dilip, at
> treasurer@egsc.seas.columbia.edu.
>
> [image: image.png]
>
> --
> *Priyanka Dilip *(any)
> *Department of Electrical Engineering*
>
> [image: Image result for egsc logo]
> EGSC Treasurer
> Engineering Graduate Student Council <http://egsc.seas.columbia.edu/ >
> Columbia University
> treasurer@egsc.seas.columbia.edu
>
>

emails.subject: Flybridge Columbia Breakfast: Connect with Startups & Talent
emails.body:
Hi All,

This is Daniel ( https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/in/danielporrasr/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WbjjzOvnVjtMF5QsxIWOXN27XmX_w0q20nfLMg7NcUpsKEsO4NpH_mcHR5xHSAtXnP6ZSojDLpaEmd0LAjds8hk$  ) from Flybridge. You previously attended or registered to our  Columbia research breakfast. I recently shared highlights ( https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/posts/danielporrasr_inside-the-room-with-columbia-university-activity-7348712645924999168-L7eM?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAABXZT0IBQfwUsHtDrJxgY4zEVxG-9CmX1_Y__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WbjjzOvnVjtMF5QsxIWOXN27XmX_w0q20nfLMg7NcUpsKEsO4NpH_mcHR5xHSAtXnP6ZSojDLpaEmd0LuSvSMd0$  ) from our discussion (on RL environments and data retrieval challenges).

We’ve been hosting these events with researchers/engineers from Columbia, MIT, and NYU, and I’ve noticed strong interest from people exploring research and engineering roles at AI startups, as well as from founders looking to hire technical talent. To help facilitate connections, I created a short form ( https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://tech-hires.replit.app/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WbjjzOvnVjtMF5QsxIWOXN27XmX_w0q20nfLMg7NcUpsKEsO4NpH_mcHR5xHSAtXnP6ZSojDLpaEmd0LanldlMo$  ) to match talent with opportunities across our 100+ portfolio companies and broader founder network.

If you’re interested in exploring opportunities at AI startups (pre-seed to Series B) or if you’re a founder looking to hire, you can fill out the form here ( https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://tech-hires.replit.app/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WbjjzOvnVjtMF5QsxIWOXN27XmX_w0q20nfLMg7NcUpsKEsO4NpH_mcHR5xHSAtXnP6ZSojDLpaEmd0LanldlMo$  ). All information *will remain confidential* and only shared when there’s a potential match. Mostly starting this to match amazing talent with amazing companies.

Best,

Daniel
